Summary: Properties of a dlnetwork?might be returned incorrectly
Description: Getting the Learnables  or State  property of a dlnetwork  returns a table with layer name, parameter name and the associated parameter value. In some cases, the layer and parameter names do not match the actual value of the parameter. Retrieving the parameter value by searching for the layer name returns the parameter value of a different layer.? This issue does not affect training or inference using a dlnetwork .

Summary: Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block outputs random number when input is zero
Description: When the input u ?to the Real Reciprocal HDL Optimized block is zero, the block outputs a random number. The expected behavior is to saturate on overflow for division by zero.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Build process might fail if the generated code contains class properties whose names match with other class names
Description: Generated C++ code might fail to build if the name of a user-defined class property matches with the name of another class. For example, consider this generated C++ code:? typedef class {}conflict;class someclass{    int conflict;    conflict b[2]; };? The property name of class  someclass matches with another class name  conflict. If you try to build the generated code, the code generator issues this error: ?error: 'conflict' does not name a type |     conflict b[2]; |           ^~~~~~~~ Compiler returned: 1? C++ compilers do not allow field names to be the same as any other field type name. The build failure happens because the code generator does not rename the fields that are of a pointer or matrix type.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: A model containing a Complex to Real-Imag block and using a mixed floating-point configuration can lead to inaccurate simulation results.
Description: If you have a model that includes a Complex to Real-Imag block and you use a mixed floating-point configuration to map your design to the native floating-point (NFP) and a vendor-specific floating-point library, all blocks in the network that contain the Complex to Real-Imag block are converted to NFP implementation and generate HDL code accordingly. The generated HDL code is valid and functions as expected. However, during testbench generation, blocks in the network that are supported by the vendor floating-point library use latency from the vendor library rather than the NFP library, which can result in discrepancies between the expected and actual values when running an HDL simulation with a simulator such as ModelSim. This is an example of the simulation result where the actual value has a delay of 10ns compared to the expected value: ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected C0D049F51D2824A4  Actual? 0000000000000000  ?#? ? Time: 120 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb  ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected 3FFB242F10A9D52E  Actual? C0D049F51D2824A4   ?#? ? Time: 130 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb  ?# ** Error: Error in Out2_im: Expected C0D049F51D2824A4 Actual? 3FFB242F10A9D52E   ?#? ? Time: 140 ns? Iteration: 1? Instance: /subsystem_tb
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds to fix the simulation results:Use NFP configuration only and do not target a vendor-specific floating-point library.Move the Complex to Real-Imag block to a subsystem.

Summary: Commented Subsystem Reference block causes Simulink to crash when compiling model
Description: If a Simulink Subsystem Reference block is commented, Simulink might crash during model compilation during sample time post propagation. The stack trace is similar to: [0] 0x00007ffa950356ea slexec::sto::rate::RateSpec::rateType  [1] 0x00007ffa95034de3 slexec::sto::rate::RateSpec::isTriggered [2] 0x00007ffa95034dba slexec::sto::rate::Rate::isTriggered?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a referenced model with a feedback loop
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a referenced model. The top model contains a feedback loop where the output of the referenced model is directly fed into its input signal. The referenced model contains a function-call subsystem whose execution is controlled by a?Trigger port. The input port inside the referenced model is configured to latch the input value and prevent it from changing during the execution of the function-call subsystem by selecting the inport block parameter?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs .?  If you generate code from a model that meets the above conditions, the code generator might eliminate temporary variables incorrectly. Incorrect elimination of local variables occurs when a function in the generated code has pointer arguments, and the caller of the function passes a memory address more than once as the pointer arguments. For example, if a top model calls a function defined in the referenced model as?botModel(&a, &b, &b) , where the memory address?&b ?is passed to the function?botModel ?twice, the generated code might be incorrect.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Order of the typedef statements in a header file might change when you regenerate code
Description: The order of the typedef ?statements in generated header files might change when you regenerate code while all ?of these conditions are met:  The model references a data type object. The data type object specifies a value for the HeaderFile  property. The data type object DataScope ?property has a value other than  Imported .   ?The changed typedef ?statement order does not affect the code functionality.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: File log data imported to the Simulation Data Inspector might be missing
Description: File log data from a Simulink Real-Time model is sporadically missing when the data is imported into the Simulation Data Inspector while the Simulation Data Inspector Window is open.
Workaround: Close the Simulation Data Inspector before importing file log data. After the file has been successfully imported, you can reopen the Simulation Data Inspector.??
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Unnecessary second initialization of internal "guard" variable
Description: The generated code for a given Simulink model may contain an internal "guard" variable when all of the following conditions are true:  The same inputs go into multiple conditional blocks(for instance if-else ladders, switch blocks, relational blocks)which branch out These branches then merge into another conditional block   The "guard" variable is initialized on declaration, and then again later, with no intervening reads or writes of the variable, like so: boolean guard1 = false;? ...? guard1 = false;
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Symbolic dimensions might stop propagating through For Each subsystem containing C Caller block
Description: Symbolic dimensions might stop propagating when the signal with symbolic dimensions passes through a For Each subsystem block via a C Caller block inside the subsystem. In the generated code, the size information of downstream blocks might be hard-coded.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: issimplified method returns true for?polyshape objects created with?Simplify?option set to false
Description: Suppose that you create a?polyshape  object using a method that accepts the Simplify ?name-value argument, which you set to?false . If you then call the issimplified  method on this polyshape  object, the method returns true .

Summary: 'X does not support locale' warning on Linux
Description: When starting MATLAB on a Linux system, the Command Window displays this warning: Warning: X does not support locale  The warning has no functional impact, so you can ignore it.

Summary: Loop induction variables of a For-loop might be incorrectly updated if the?for-loop does not execute
Description: Incorrect code might generate for a MATLAB function that meets all  of these conditions:? The MATLAB function contains a for -loop with induction variables. The upper and lower bounds of the induction variables are specified in the loop. There is a possibility of the loop not getting executed at run-time.  Consider the following MATLAB code: function [k,m] = r(s,e)    k = int32(0);    m = int32(zeros(1,2048));    for idx = s : e        k = k + 1;        m(k) = idx;    endend? MATLAB Coder generates the following code snippet:?? for (idx = s; idx <= e; idx++) {    m[idx - s] = idx;  }  *k = (e - s) + 1; Notice that if the for -loop does not execute at run-time, the variable k  will hold an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: After running clear classes or clear functions certain features no longer open
Description: After running the clear classes  or clear functions  commands, certain windows do not open. These features include App Designer , UIFigure , Add-On Explorer , Simulink Start Page , Simulink Data Explorer  and others. A window for the feature briefly appears and then disappears.
Workaround: Run this command at MATLAB startup or add to the startup.m file:setenv('MW_CEF_ENABLE_BINARY_TRANSPORT','0');

Summary: Error during code generation when model contains multiple lookup tables with table values from an input port
Description: An error occurs during code generation of an AUTOSAR component model that contains multiple Interpolation using Prelookup or Direct Lookup Table blocks when one of the following conditions are true: The lookup table values come from an input port. The lookup table values come from?a Constant block whose values are?defined by a?Simulink.Parameter  object.   If either of these conditions are true, the following error is produced during code generation. The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook, during the after_tlc hook generated the following error: Not enough input arguments.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Image Resizer HDL code returns incorrect output pixel values when performing a horizontal-only resize
Description: This issue applies to the Image Resizer block and the visionhdl.ImageResizer System object. The simulation behaviour of the Image Resizer block and the visionhdl.ImageResizer System object is correct. The generated HDL code may return incorrect output pixel values when InputSize height == OutputSize height, such as a resize from [48, 64] to [48, 48].  The presence and severity of pixel output errors depends on the vertical contrast in the input data, the fixed-point rounding method, and the horizontal resize scale factor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel with row majority and non-commutative operation
Description: Code generated for gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel ?? might produce incorrect results if both  these conditions are satisfied:  The function that calls   gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel uses row-major layout.   The function handle passed to   gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel is non-commutative.    For example, the issue affects the generated GPU code for this MATLAB function: function C = entryPoint(A, B)% A and B are numeric arrayscoder.rowMajor;func = @(x,y) x-y;C = gpucoder.matrixMatrixKernel(func, A, B);end

Summary: datcomimport?function for cases with HORIZONTAL STABILIZER COEFFICIENTS swap CL and CD?values
Description: When calculating untrimmed and trimmed coefficients for a movable horizontalstabilizer,?the?datcompimport? function swaps the values for the struct variables cd_tailutrim  ?and cl_tailutrim  ?and the values for cd_tailtrim  ?and cl_tailtrim  .
Workaround: After running datcomimport for an affected case, swap the values for variables cd_tailutrim?and cl_tailutrim?and variables cd_tailtrim?and cl_tailtrim.

Summary: The movmedian method of fillmissing2 rounds negative integers incorrectly
Description: When MATLAB calculates the median of integer-type data and the median lies between two integer values, MATLAB rounds away from zero.  >>M = median(int32([1 2]))  M =    int32       2    >>M = median(int32([-1 -2]))  M =    int32       -2    ?However, the movmedian  method of fillmissing2  incorrectly rounds negative integers towards zero. >>F = fillmissing2(int32([-1 0 -2]),"movmedian",3,MissingLocations=[false true false]) F =      1ˇÁ3 int32?row vector        -1   -1   -2
Workaround: Avoid this issue by casting integer-type data to double before calling fillmissing2, then casting the result to the original type.>>A =? int32([-1 0 -2]);>>F = fillmissing2(double(A),"movmedian",3,MissingLocations=[false true false]);>>F = int32(F)F=  1ˇÁ3 int32?row vector   -1   -2   -2

Summary: Generate HDL code for MATLAB Function block that contains the function?reinterpretcast might have simulation mismatch
Description: HDL code generated from a MATLAB Function block that contains the function ?reinterpretcast  might have unnecessary saturation logic that results in simulation mismatch. For example, this MATLAB code might show a mismatch in simulation results with the generated HDL code : out = reinterpretcast((fi(input,16,1),numerictype(1,16,15));
Workaround: Wrap the casting value using the?fi function and set the?OverflowAction?property to Wrap by using this command:fi(input, 'OverflowAction', 'Wrap')
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: External mode simulation with C++ target application fails
Description: An external mode simulation might fail if the model has this combination of configuration parameter settings: Language  ? is  C++  Use dynamic memory allocation for model initialization  check box is selected
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Parallel tasks that use mpiexec might fail on Linux clusters without ˇ°libunwindˇ± installed
Description: When you use MATLAB Parallel Server? integrated to a Linux cluster that uses a third-party scheduler like SLURM, and the cluster machines do not have ˇ°libunwindˇ± installed, parallel tasks that use?mpiexec ?might fail to run with a message that refers to "libunwind.so.8 ".? For example, cluster validation might fail with an error like the following: /network/installs/matlab/r2023a/bin/glnxa64/mpiexec: error while loading shared libraries: libunwind.so.8: Cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
Workaround: Install "libunwind" on the cluster machines. For example, on systems that use apt-get, use this command:sudo apt-get install -y libunwind-dev?

Summary: Configuring memory sections in the code mappings for model parameter arguments has no impact on generated code
Description: In the Data Defaults  tab of the Code Mappings editor, when you configure the Memory Section  property of the storage class for model parameter arguments, there is no effect on the generated code.
Workaround: To configure memory sections for model parameter arguments, define a storage class and apply the memory section to the storage class. To use?const?or?volatile?qualifiers, configure the properties of the storage class.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Passing global variables of unnamed struct or enum type to custom code functions my produce incorrect error in C Function block.
Description: Your simulation may produce an incorrect mismatch error if the following is true: Your C Function block has a custom code function call that accepts global variables of unnamed struct  or enum  type  For example, consider the following custom code header file: // foo.h   struct myStruct {? ? ? ? int p;  };  extern struct myStruct gVar;  int myFcn(const struct myStruct *in);  Inside the C Function block,? y = myFcn(&gVar);  simulating the model produces the following incorrect error: Passing argument 1 of external C function 'myFcn' in the C Function block 'myModel/C Function' from incompatible pointer type. Ensure the pointer argument is compatible with the external C function's parameter type. Occurred in line:  y = myFcn(&gVar);
Workaround: If possible, name the structs and enums in the custom code.

Summary: Incorrect execution of timed events in Stateflow charts that are inside function-call subsystems with multiple initiators having different sample times
Description: Stateflow absolute time operators such as at , before , every , or after  may incorrectly evaluate to true when all the following conditions are met:? Stateflow chart is inside a function-call subsystem that has multiple initiators Function-call initiators have different sample times, causing the function-call subsystem to run at the union of those sample times
Workaround: Set the same sample time on all initiators of the parent function-call subsystem.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a nested bus whose innermost bus fields are updated through guided specifications
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a nested bus containing multiple hierarchies. For example,  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?struct {?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? int val;  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? }? SubBusA  ? ? ? ? ? struct {  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? subBusA a  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? subBusA b?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? int c }? BusA; The fields of the innermost bus are updated inplace by using one of these guided specifications: Defining a MATLAB function interface in the form of y = foo(y) . Defining a Reusable  storage class and using the same variable name for the input and output of the update operation. Using function prototype control to specify the same name for the input and output of the update operation when the input and output of the update operation are the root input and output, respectively.   If your model satisfies the above conditions, for a defined nested bus X ? of type?BusA , the code generator might incorrectly eliminate an assignment like the following: ? ? ? ? ? ?X.a.val = X.b.val;?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Generated HDL code for the Reciprocal block may have one bit simulation mismatch when using?ShiftAdd?architecture and custom output types.?
Description: HDL code generated for Reciprocal block or Math Function Reciprocal block?might result in a simulation mismatch of one bit when you set the following conditions for the blocks: Architecture  is set to ShiftAdd  Output data type  is selected other than? Inherit: Inherit via internal rule  Max(Input word length, Output?word length)  is equal to  Sum ( Input fractional length, Output fractional length, 1)
Workaround: Increase the Input or Output word length by one bit so that it meets following criteria:?? Max(Input word length, Output?word length)?must be one bit greater than?Sum(Input fractional length, Output fractional length, 1)And use Data Type Conversion block after the Reciprocal block or Math Function Reciprocal block to reduce the word length by one bit.?

Summary: The?medicalref3d object might specify incorrect patient positions in world coordinates
Description: The?medicalref3d  object and its object functions might specify incorrect patient positions in the world coordinate system under these conditions: The?intrinsicToWorld ,?intrinsicToWorldMapping ,?oneSliceIntrinsicToWorldMapping ,?worldToIntrinsic ,? and?worldToSubscript ?object functions might return transformed coordinates that are incorrectly shifted several millimeters in the patient coordinate system. This impacts both the forward (intrinsic to world) and backward (world to intrinsic) mappings. The?medicalref3d  object might specify incorrect spatial referencing for volumes with unequal pixel spacing in the X and Y directions. This behavior can occur if you create a medicalref3d  object directly by using the syntax?medicalref3d(volumeSize,position,pixelSpacing,cosines) , or if you create a?medicalVolume  object by reading a DICOM file whose metadata specifies unequal pixel spacing.

Summary: AutoSAR access function calls might be generated for zero initialization of output ports even when Remove zero initialization for root I/O is selected
Description: The code generator might generate AutoSAR access function calls for zero initialization of output ports when the Remove zero initialization for root I/O  configuration parameter?is selected. For example, consider this generated code: void model11_Init(void)  {  ??(void)Rte_Write_out1_out1(model1.out1);  }   The generated code initializes?model1.out1  despite the Remove zero initialization for root I/O ?configuration parameter .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Sine Wave block with discrete sample time inside If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block might output wrong answer?in normal simulation mode
Description: The output value of a Sine Wave block with its?Sine type ?set to Time based  and satisfying? all of the following conditions might not match the absolute time in normal simulation mode: The Sine Wave block is inside an If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The action subsystem is nested inside another conditionally executed subsystem. The Sine Wave block has discrete?sample time. The action subsystem is not configured to reset when the subsystem reenables. The action subsystem might be disabled because its parent subsystem is disabled, although the action signals remain the same.  This issue only affects simulation in normal mode. Generated code is correct.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 4, R2023b

Summary: Entropy weight optimizer of rlSACAgent is reset when the agent is saved during training
Description: The entropy weight used in rlSACAgent  controls the balance between exploitation and exploration. It is automatically adjusted if LearnRate  of EntropyWeightOptions  is positive. The agent uses an optimizer to update the entropy weight. However, this optimizer is incorrectly reset every time the agent is saved during training (the agent is saved when SaveAgentCriteria  of rlTrainingOptionsis  is different than none ). Specifically, when the agent is saved, a new entropy weight optimizer is created using the?Algorithm ?and OptimizerParameters ?properties in?EntropyWeightOptions , hence any internal information in the optimizer from the previous episode is discarded. As a consequence, the training results will differ between a training session in which the agent is not saved and one in which the agent is saved.
Workaround: You can use one of the following workarounds to avoid the issue:Do not save the agent during training. Set SaveAgentCriteria and SaveAgentValue in rlTrainingOptions to none.Use a fixed entropy weight. Set LearnRate of EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions to 0, and adjust EntropyWeight (default value is 1) in EntropyWeightOptions of rlSACAgentOptions. Try 0.5, 0.2, or 0.1 for?EntropyWeight,?and increase this value if the agent needs more exploration, or reduce this value if more agent exploitation is desired.Use rlTD3Agent instead of rlSACAgent. TD3 agents are generally competitive with SAC agents.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder can generate incorrect code from a MATLAB Function block that contains multidimensional indexing using?end keyword
Description: When you generate HDL code using either of these options: A MATLAB Function block that has the HDL block property Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath  A MATLAB function with the Aggressive Dataflow Conversion  parameter enabled in the?Optimizations ?tab of the HDL Code Generation  task in the MATLAB HDL Workflow Advisor  then you may get incorrect code if you use end  in multidimensional indexing. For example, the following expression may generate incorrect code: x(1, end)

Summary: Y-Junction(IL, TL) blocks calculate loss coefficients incorrectly for IdelˇŻchik correlation
Description: When the Loss coefficient model  parameter is set to IdelˇŻchikcorrelation , the loss coefficients are off by a factor equal to the square of the ratio ofthe branch and common channel flow velocities.

Summary: polyspace-configure fails with?error: Invalid cross-device link
Description: if you run polyspace-configure  on Linux, the command might fail when copying files from the Polyspace installation folder to the system temporary folder (/tmp ) and you see an error message similar to this: ERROR: Could not copy "<installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test" to "/tmp/polyspace_configure_tmp-.../target.cpp": Invalid cross-device link  The issue occurs when the Polyspace installation folder and the /tmp  folder are mounted on two different filesystems, and has been observed with NFSv4.2.
Workaround: To work around this issue try one of these options:Install Polyspace on the same filesystem as the /tmp folder, or use the option -tmp-path to specify a temporary folder path that points to a location that is on the same filesystem as the Polyspace installation folder. For instance:polyspace-configure -tmp-path <path on same filesystem as installation> ...Create a folder on the same filesystem as your Polyspace installation, manually copy files to that folder, and use the option -tmp-path to specify the path to that folder:mkdir mytmpcp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/target.ccp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/target.cppcp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/sniff_implicit_includes.ccp <installation folder>/matlab/polyspace/configure/test_file/test mytmp/sniff_implicit_includes.cpppolyspace-configure -tmp-path mytmp -keep-all-files ...Option?-keep-all-files keeps the temporary folder instead of deleting it after each run. When specify this option,?polyspace-configure?skips the copying of files after the first run.

Summary: HDL code generation fails for Complex Divide HDL Optimized block when input data type is built-in integer
Description: HDL code generation fails when using the Complex Divide HDL Optimized block with this error: Non-synthesizable single or double data type found in HDL code,  at Function 'eml_mtimes_helper' (#33554449. 2253. 2263),  line 76,  column 13 Function 'Complex Divide HDL Optimized/ ForEach - Complex Divide/ Rotate to real/ Rotate to real' (#1156. 1738. 1799),  line 37,  column 20.  To fix this problem,  cast the floating-point data type to a fixed-point data type.?
Workaround: In the?Rotate to real?block, modify the setting Treat these inherited Simulink signal types as fi objects from 'Fixed-point' to 'Fixed-point & Integer'.1. Break the library link of the?Complex Divide HDL Optimized block:    a. Right-click on the block. Select?Library Link > Disable Link.    b. Right-click on the block again. Select?Library Link?>?Break Link. 2. Navigate to the?Rotate to real?block.a. Right-click the Complex Divide HDL Optimized block. Select Mask > Look Under Mask.b. Open the ForEach - Complex Divide subsystem.c. Open the Rotate to real subsystem.3. Right-click on the Rotate to real block and select Explore to open the Model?Explorer. 4. Change the setting Treat these inherited Simulink signal types as fi objects from 'Fixed-point'?to 'Fixed-point & Integer'.When updating to a newer version of MATLAB, the Complex Divide HDL Optimized block with the library link removed needs to be replaced with the new version of the Complex Divide HDL Optimized block with?the library link in place to incorporate the updates.

Summary: isShared == aFcn->isShared()?assertion when generating code
Description: The code generator might throw an error if you generate code from a Simulink model that meets all of the following conditions: The model?contains MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks that have subfunctions The subfunctions are not inlined. The model is configured to place its utility code in a shared location, or you generate model reference code from the model (for example, if you call slbuild(myModel,'ModelReferenceCoderTarget') ?to generate the code)  The error resembles the following: Code generation assertion 'isShared == aFcn->isShared()' failed?

Summary: Functions specklefilt and imregicp from Medical Imaging Toolbox error when used in deployed applications.
Description: Functions specklefilt  and imregicp  throw an error when used in deployed applications (.exe).  For example, the specklefilt  function, when used in a deployed application, throws the following error: Unable to resolve the name 'medical.internal.builtins.specklefilt_halide'. ?

Summary: External test harness specified as top-level model?in Test Manager System Under Test crashes MATLAB
Description: For a test case, if you specify an external test harness as a top-level model in the System Under Test  section of the Test Manager, running the test crashes MATLAB. The crash might produce logs that contain information similar to: slCheckForClose at B:\matlab\src\sl_graphical_classes\SLRootBD.cpp:3793 (in D:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_graphical_classes.dll)  sl::simharness::SlHarnessManagerImpl::canBeDeactivated at? D:\matlab\toolbox\shared\simulinktest\src\simharness\mgr\SlHarnessManagerImpl.cpp:1359 (in? D:\matlab\bin\win64\simharness.dll)    sl::simharness::SlHarnessManagerImpl::deactivateHarness at? D:\matlab\toolbox\shared\simulinktest\src\simharness\mgr\SlHarnessManagerImpl.cpp:1474 (in D:\matlab\bin\win64\simharness.dll)
Workaround: To test an external test harness with the Test Manager, specify the harness owner model in the Model box of the System Under Test?section, and specify the harness model in the Harness?box of the Test Harness?section.To use the programmatic workflow to define a test case, use sltest.testmanager.testcase.setproperty?to define the HarnessOwner as the harness owner model, and the HarnessName as the test harness being tested.

Summary: Model Advisor Check Compliance metrics report incorrect check result status for child models
Description: When a Model Advisor Check Compliance metric executes on a model that references other models, the metric can report an incorrect result status for the checks run on the child model. Model Advisor Check Compliance metrics include the Model Advisor Check Compliance metric for High-Integrity Systems, the Model Advisor Check Compliance metric for MAB, and custom metrics that use the family ID mathworks.metrics.ModelAdvisorCheckCompliance .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: polyspace-configure unable to extract compiler configuration
Description: When you run polyspace-configure  to extract your build configuration, and use gcc  or a gcc -based compiler, Polyspace cannot compute the compiler predefined macros, implicit include folders, or target characteristics. The command console output or log indicates a message similar to this: ERROR: Compiler error when trying to build a simple file before sniffing implicit includes WARNING: The predefined include folder could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project. WARNING: The target characteristics could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project.WARNING: The predefined macros could not be computed and might result in an incomplete project.   These issues occur because?polyspace-configure  incorrectly parses the compiler command option --sysroot .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, open file?<polyspaceRoot>/configure/compiler_configuration/gcc.xml in a text editor and replace<opt numArgs="1">-sysroot</opt>with:<opt numArgs="1">--sysroot</opt>where, <polyspaceRoot> is your Polyspace installation folder, for instance,?C:\Program Files\Polyspace\R2022b.

Summary: Incorrect results for a model containing an Observer Reference when both the models have?floor or ceil rounding operations
Description: When a model containsan Observer Reference ?block such that both the design model as well as the observermodel logic involves performing floor  and/or ceil  truncating operations, thenSimulink Design Verifier analysis on the design model may ignore suchoperations on one of the two models thereby providing incorrect analysisresults.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a

Summary: Thermostatic Expansion Valve (2P) results in compilation error when the Valve parameterization parameter is Tabulated data - quadrant diagram and the Bulb temperature dynamics parameter is On
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Thermostatic Expansion Valve (2P) where the Valve parameterization ?parameter is Tabulated data - quadrant diagram  and the Bulb temperature dynamics  parameter is On  results in the following error message: ?Invalid use of non-parametric value 'T_evap'.  All entities referenced in this location must be parameters.?

Summary: Generating PDF report containing nonprinting control characters in document heading or caption crashes MATLAB
Description: If you generate a PDF report that contains nonprinting control characters in a document heading, the following error occurs and MATLAB crashes. Unsupported control characters include characters with ASCII codes between 1 and 31, except horizontal tab (ASCII code 9), line feed (ASCII code 10), and carriage return (ASCII code 13). Error using mlreportgen.internal.fop.FOPObject/executeCannot convert "C:/workspace/root.fo" to "C:/workspace/test.pdf". Reason: Invalid Unicode character: U+001B? ? If the nonprinting character appears in a table of contents or list of captioned report elements, such as figure captions or table titles, MATLAB crashes.? For example, the following code adds the escape character to a document. MATLAB crashes when the document is closed. d = mlreportgen.dom.Document("output");append(d,mlreportgen.dom.TOC);append(d,mlreportgen.dom.Heading1(char(27))); close(d);
Workaround: Remove unsupported characters from the input string before adding the string to a DOM component or document. For example,inputChar = ['line feed: ',char(10),'escape character: ',char(27)];supportedChars = [9, 10, 13]; % Horizontal tab, line feed, and carriage return are supportedtoRemove = ~ismember(inputChar,supportedChars) & (inputChar < 32);inputChar(toRemove) = []
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong code generated when?Simulink.Signal object is attached to string signal
Description: Simulink.Signal  does not support string ?data type. However, Simulink fails to return an error if a Simulink.Signal  object with DataType  set to?auto ?is attached to a string signal in a model. Instead, the generated code produces wrong answers.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: C++ compilation error due to assignment operations to this pointer
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate assignment operations to this  pointer when generating C++ code from MATLAB code that satisfies all  of these conditions: The MATLAB code contains a handle class. The handle class has a non-static class method. The class method contains an arguments block  . The first argument declaration in the?arguments?block  ?includes a specification of the size of the argument.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef MyClass < handle  ??? ...   ??? methods   ??? ...   ??????? function Foo(obj)  ? ????????? arguments   ? ? ? ????????? obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}  ? ? ? ????? end   ? ????????? ...   ??????? end   ??? end   end   MATLAB Coder generates this C++ code for the above MATLAB code:   void MyClass::Foo()  {  ??? this = handle_init();  ??? ...  }   In the generated code,? the first argument of the non-static class method in the MATLAB code is represented as?this  pointer, ?and an assignment operation happens to the pointer.?  Assignment to this  pointer ?is not allowed in C++. MSVC gives the following error message: error C2106: '=': left operand must be l-value  GCC gives the following error message: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment   Clang gives the following error message:  error: expression is not assignable
Workaround: Remove the size specification from argument declarations in the arguments block.? In the MATLAB code example given above, replacing the code line obj (1, 1) {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")}?with?obj {mustBeA(obj, "MyClass")} resolves the error.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 5, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?predict?function might produce incorrect results if 'CU' inputs are passed to dlnetwork
Description: Code generated for the dlnework ?object function?predict  might produce incorrect results if all  of these conditions are true:? Target library is 'cudnn '. The dlnetwork  does not have any input layer. For example, it has been initialized with example inputs. The data format of the?dlarray  you pass to predict ?is 'CU '.  Here is an example workflow that produces an incorrect answers: % Create example entry point function 'mpredict'function out = mpredict(matFile, in) %#codegennet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork(matFile);out = predict(net, in);end% Define dlnetwork objectexampleInput = dlarray(ones(5,'single'),'CU');dlnet = dlnetwork(reluLayer, exampleInput);save('trainedNet.mat','dlnet');% Define input types to entry-point function 'mpredict'I = randi(255,[5,5]);dlIn1 = dlarray(single(I),'CU');Inputs = {coder.Constant('trainedNet.mat'), dlIn1};% Generate codecfg = coder.gpuConfig('mex');cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('cudnn');codegen mpredict -config cfg -args Inputs
Workaround: Do either of these:When specifying function input types in the codegen command, change the data format from 'CU' to 'CB'.?Change target library from 'cudnn' to 'tensorrt'.?

Summary: Code generation errors if manipulatorRRT uses?rigidBodyTree?with data format value other than row
Description: Code generation errors if a MATLAB function creates a manipulatorRRT   object with?the input rigidBodyTree ?having a value other than row  (say struct ?or column ) for the?DataFormat ?property.? For example, this MATLAB function fails to generate code.  ?? %%foo.m ??  function y=foo()  ??  %#codegen ??  rbt=twoJointRigidBodyTree("column");  ??  planner=manipulatorRRT(rbt, {}); ??  y=plan(planner, [0, 0], [0, 0]);  ??  end  The error message from ">> codegen foo " in MATLAB is,   ---  Nontunable property 'DataFormatInternal' may only be assigned once.    Error in ==> RigidBodyTree Line: 2267 Column: 13   Code generation failed: To view the report,  open('codegen/ mex/ foo/ html/ report. mldatx')    Error using codegen   ---  This happens because the?DataFormat ?property of the input?rigidBodyTree,  rbt,  is not "row"  (in this case, on line 4 ?in foo.m, it   is "column")  .
Workaround: Set the DataFormat property of the rigidBodyTree object to row?before creating the manipulatorRRT object.This pattern shows the workaround:?? % Load or construct a rigid body tree in "row" data format. ?? rbt=loadrobot(...,DataFormat="row");?? planner=manipulatorRRT(rbt,...);

Summary: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code containing loops with the same number of iterations that might conditionally write to the same scalar value
Description: Incorrect code might generate from the MATLAB code that meets all  of these conditions: The code contains for -loops or while  loops or a combination of loops that have the same number of iterations.? The loops write to the same scalar variable. One of the writes is conditional, where the output of the condition varies across loop iterations.?  This happens because of an optimization that incorrectly fuses loops of the above MATLAB coding pattern. Consider this example MATLAB: ? ? ?function y = example(a,b,y)  ? ? ? ? for i = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2 = b(6:1:6,4:1:4);? ? ? ? ? ? ??  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? for j = 1:7  ? ? ? ? ? ? if (a((j) + (6),1)) >= (double(0))  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? y(1).f2(1:1:1,1:1:1) = abs(int16(0));  ? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  The for- loops in the MATLAB code have the same number of iterations and both write to y(1).f2 . T he second loop conditionally writes to it based on the if -condition. The generated C code looks like this: ? ?void example(const short a[13], const short b[81], struct0_T y[2])  ? ?{  ? ? short b_i;  ? ? for (int i{0}; i < 7; i++) {  ? ? ? b_i = b[32];  ? ? ? if (a[i + 6] >= 0) {  ? ? ? ? b_i = 0;  ? ? ?} } ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i;  ? ? y[0].f2 = b_i; } The generate code incorrectly fused the MATLAB for -loops which produces incorrect results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when a referenced model contains Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks connected in series
Description: Incorrect simulation results are possible in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. The referenced model contains two Bus Creator blocks and two Bus Selector blocks. The outputs of the Bus Creator blocks are of the same data type. One Bus Selector block chooses an element from the first Bus Creator block and passes it to the second Bus Creator block as an input. The other Bus Selector block chooses an element from the second Bus Creator block. The index of this element is higher than the index of the element chosen by the first Bus Selector block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Motor & Drive (System-Level) block inaccurately extends tabulated efficiency into other quadrants
Description: If you set the  Parameterize losses by  parameter to  Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed andtorque   and you provide data for only one or two quadrants?(the values of the  Vector ofspeeds (w) for tabulated losses  or  Vector of torques (T) fortabulated losses  parameters are only positive), the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block extends the full four-quadrant efficiency map inaccurately.  Consequently, if you run a simulation where the motor operates in a quadrant in which you did not specify the efficiency table, the power that the motor dissipates might be inaccurate.
Workaround: The best practice is to provide tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque, rather than tabulated efficiency data, because:Efficiency becomes ill-defined for zero speed or zero torque.Using losses, you can also account for fixed losses that are still present for zero speed or torque.To fix the quadrant extension issue, provide tabulated loss data instead of efficiency data:Set the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated loss data as a function of speed and torque.Convert the efficiency map into an equivalent loss map. Use the attached function?getExtendedLossTableFromEffTable.m or compute the equivalent loss map from first principles.Set the Corresponding losses, P(w,T)?parameter value to the resulting loss table.To automatically set the block parameters, use the attached function convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m.In your model, click the Motor & Drive (System-Level) block that you want to convert.At the MATLAB Command Window, enter:? >> convertEffToLossInMotorBlock(gcb)If you set?the?Parameterize losses by?parameter to?Tabulated efficiency data as a function of speed, torque, and DC supply voltage,?you must modify the attached function?convertEffToLossInMotorBlock.m?accordingly.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results when you select the latch option for a function-call model input port that is in a feedback path
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all  of these?conditions are met: In the referenced model, there is a Trigger Port block of root level of the model with the?Trigger type ?parameter set to function-call . In the referenced model, there are root-level Inport blocks with the?Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs ?parameter selected. In the top model, the corresponding input port of the Model block is on the feedback signal path.?
Workaround: Clear the Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs?option on the referenced model root level Inport blocks and insert Function-Call Feedback Latch blocks in front of corresponding input ports of the Model block in the top model.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when storage classes used by the input and output signals of a block have different Preserve array dimensions property setting
Description: Incorrect code might generate for a model that meets all  of these conditions:The model contains a signal?using a storage class that is configured to preserve array dimensions. The model contains a global or input/output signal?using a storage class that is not configured to preserve array dimensions. One of these signals is an input to a block and the other one is the output from the same block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: duration operator returns incorrect results in some atomic subcharts
Description: The?duration ?operator in an atomic subchart might return incorrect results if the following conditions are true: The atomic subchart has a periodic sample rate The atomic subchart has an input or output data that is mapped to a main chart data with a different scope OR has a data that is mapped to one bus field or array element of a larger main chart data The duration  operator uses some data mapped to the main chart. Note that the data the duration  operator uses does not have to be one of the data from the previous condition.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if a chain of Bus Creator blocks passes across subsystem boundaries and connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all  of these conditions:? The model contains an atomic subsystem and a chain of Bus Creator blocks. The chain of Bus Creator blocks passes across the subsystem boundary to get inside of the subsystem. Inside the subsystem, the output of the chain connects to a Unit Delay block.  This happens because of an optimization that incorrectly reuses variables for the outputs of Bus Creator and Unit Delay blocks.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:??Insert a Signal Conversion block between the last Bus Creator block of the chain and Unit Delay block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter.In the model's Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization pane, set the Optimize global data access to None.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Clock-rate pipelining enabled on subsystems inside the DUT and not globally can cause a simulation failure
Description: A Modelsim simulation failure can occur when the model configuration parameter?Clock-rate pipelining  ?is disabled but Oversampling  set to greater than 1 and these conditions are met for a subsystem inside the DUT: The subsystem has the HDL block property ClockRatePipelining  ?set to on.  An input pipeline is introduced before the subsystem. Sharing or streaming is enabled for the subsystem.

MICRO_FPGA_SOC?   HSP Setup Wizard  is not supported for Microchip Devices.?
Workaround: Select?Setup Later option appearing on HSP Setup Wizard dialog box.

Summary: Red Non-Terminating Call (NTC) check on stubbed functions with parameters of type "pointer to const structure"
Description: If your code has a stubbed function that takes a pointer to a const  structure, for example, ? void foo(const S*) where S  is a structure containing pointer fields, you might see a red Non-Terminating Call (NTC) check when you call this function with a partially initialized structure. Despite the partial initialization, the red check is not appropriate since the stubbed function might not read the non-initialized fields. Note that a function is stubbed when its body is not available to the Polyspace analysis. You can also explicitly stub functions using the option?-functions-to-stub.  For example: typedef struct {  int x;  int* p;} S;extern void foo(const S*);int main() {     S s;    s.x = 42;    foo(&s);  // Here s.p is not initialized, there is a red check on this call}

Summary: Code Prover analysis generates Bad instr error
Description: The following specific C++ construction leads to a?Bad instr ?error during Code Prover analysis:  You instantiate a template where the template parameter is a pointer to a function that takes non-const  parameters. The instantiation happens with a pointer to a function that takes?const  parameters.  The error occurs because of the conversion (cast) required during the template instantiation.  For example, the template below is initialized with a pointer to a function foo()  that takes a const float ?parameter: template <void (*pFcn)(float)>void callFcn() {    pFcn(2.0f);}void foo(const float);int main() {    callFcn<foo>();    return 0;}
Workaround: To work around this issue, instantiate the template with a pointer to a function that takes non-const parameters. For instance, in the previous example, you can change the prototype of the function used to instantiate the template as follows:void foo(float);

Summary: Segmentation violation when updating Simulink model containing multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when updating a Simulink model that contains multiple signals specified by Simulink.Signal  objects driving a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block. ?Your model might be affected when all the following conditions are true: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem such as enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem contains an Outport block with Initial output  set to []  (empty matrix). The Outport block is driven by a Mux block or a virtual Bus Creator block. The source signal for the second input and above of the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The storage class (CoderInfo.StorageClass ) of the Simulink.Signal  object in the model workspace has a value other than 'Auto' . Initial value (InitialValue) ?of the Simulink.Signal  object is not empty.  A segmentation violation is more likely with higher number of input ports for the Mux block or virtual Bus Creator block.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Generated code with large identifiers can be incompatible with generated code test generation
Description: When the generated code for a model is containing C identifiers with more than 63 chars, generated code test generation is only using the first 63 chars to identify them. This can cause an error if different identifiers are truncated to the same name, causing the model to be incompatible.  That error will be reported as An internal error occurred during the code analysis  before the incompatibility message.
Workaround: A workaround is to reduce the length of the identifiers in the generated code so that they are less than 63 chars long. This can be done by:Reducing the value of Maximum identifier length in the Code Generation / Identifiers setting, and/orUsing a shorter name for the model name

Description: Running normal mode simulation of a model with this configuration might crash MATLAB or produce wrong answers: The model contains a Data Store Memory block (with corresponding Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks) or a Bus Assignment block. One of the input signals to these blocks is grounded or unconnected. The data type of this input signal is a bus type that contains string elements. The dimension of this input signal is not scalar.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect when a Bus Creator block is connected to the virtual?outport of a conditional subsystem
Description: Generated code might be incorrect?for a model that meets all ?of these conditions: The model contains a conditional subsystem The conditional subsystem contains a Bus Creator block The conditional subsystem contains an outport block In the outport block parameters, Ensure output is virtual  is selected The output of the Bus Creator block is connected to the outport block

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Enumeration values passed as inputs during MEX execution when the MEX was originally generated to accept integer types
Description: In MATLAB, an enumeration class is derivedfrom one of the allowed integer class types. Suppose that:? When generating a MEX from your MATLAB source code, you specified the input to be of an integer type (for example, uint8 ), and   At run time, you pass an enumeration value that derives from the same integer type (for example,?uint8 ) ?as the input to this MEX.?    In such situations, the MEX execution might produce incorrect results. For example, consider the following MATLAB function foo  and the enumeration class WeekDays  that is derived from the uint8  integer class. function output = foo(input)output = input;endclassdef WeekDays < uint8    enumeration        Monday(0),        Tuesday(1),        Wednesday(2),        Thursday(3),        Friday(4)    endend Generate a MEX for foo . Specify the input to be of uint8  scalar type.  codegen foo -args {uint8(0)}  If you call foo_mex ?by passing a WeekDays  enumeration value as the input argument, the MEX might produce an incorrect result. >> foo_mex(WeekDays.Monday)ans =  uint8   17
Workaround: During MEX execution, pass an input of the same type that you specified during MEX generation. For example, cast the enumeration value to an integer before passing it to the generated MEX.

Summary: Data does not update in deployed DSP System Toolbox scope applications
Description: When you run a deployed application (.exe, .app) that contains timescope, dsp.ArrayPlot, or spectrumAnalyzer objects, the data on the visual does not update for these post simulation operations: 1) Zooming or panning on the axes 2) Clicking on the Fit-to-view button  3) Turning on measurements

Summary: When a linear transfer function containing input or IO delays is used in the nlhw command, the delays are ignored in the resulting model
Description: The nlhw  command allows you to use a linear transfer function to initialize the model's linear component as an alternative to specifying model orders directly. When you specify a? linear transfer function using an idtf  model containing non-zero values for either the InputDelay or the IODelay property or both, the resulting Hammerstein-Wiener model ignores these delay values.
Workaround: Convert the idtf model to an equivalent idpoly model. For example, replace:sys = nlhw(data, linmod, ...)?where linmod is an idtf object, with:?sys = nlhw(data, idpoly(linmod), ...)?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Selecting?Remove root level I/O zero initialization yields incorrect code when both top and bottom models have Merge blocks connected to outports and the outport of the bottom model is connected to the Merge block in the top model
Description: Selecting?Remove root level I/O zero initialization  yields incorrect code generation?when?all ?of the following are true:The top model has a Merge block with a zero initial output The top model Merge block is connected to an outport The top model has a bottom model whose outport is connected to the top model Merge block The bottom model has a Merge block with a nonzero initial output The bottom model Merge block is connected to an outport  For example, consider this code, generated with?Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?unselected: ?/* Model initialize function */  void top_initialize(void)  {        /* SystemInitialize for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model' incorporates:        *  Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top'        */       bot_Init(&top_Y.Outport_top);       /* SystemInitialize for Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top' incorporates:        *  Merge: '<Root>/Merge'        */       top_Y.Outport_top = 0.0;          }?    Compare to this incorrect code, generated with Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?selected: void top_initialize(void)   {    /* SystemInitialize for ModelReference: '<Root>/Model' incorporates:    * Outport: '<Root>/Outport_top'    */    bot_Init(&top_Y.Outport_top);     }?  In the incorrect code, the function bot_Init  might set?top_Y.Outport_top  to a nonzero value. This yields different results from the code generated with Remove root level I/O zero initialization ?selected.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Exported ARXML does not contain SwDataDefProps for inlined elements of structure implementation data types
Description: The ARXML exported upon model build is missing SwDataDefProps  for inlined elements of the ImplementationDataType  structure type. This might occur when: The model is created using an ARXML file generated using a third-party AUTOSAR authoring tool. Elements of the ImplementationDataType  structure type?are inlined in the model.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation gives wrong answer when models contain Simulink functions, and those Simulink functions are called from Initialize function inside a referenced sample time independent model
Description: Accelerator mode simulation may give incorrect results when all of the following conditions are met: There is at least a 2-level model hierarchy with the referenced model being a sample time independent model. The referenced model contains a function-caller block inside an Initialize function subsystem. The function-caller block calls a simulink function in the parent model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect backfolding of variable assignment into function calls
Description: In a MATLAB function, when a global variable is declared and used in only one MATLAB function and the functions that it calls, it might be converted to a local variable and passed into the called function as an input. Code generation might incorrectly fold back the assignment that copies the value of the variable into the call site of the called function. This incorrect backfolding results in a change of evaluation order and incorrect results. For example, this MATLAB function calls the function callee : function out = caller(in1, in2)?  ? ? global P2. ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (1) Declaration of global variable P2  ? ? [P1] = callee(in1,in2);? ? ? ?// (2) Callsite of function callee , which directly access global P2  ? ? P1(1,1) = P2;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?// (3) Copy of P2  end?  ?In the generated code, the variable P2  is converted to a local variable and passed into callee  as an input in statement (2). The copy of P2  in statement (3) is folded back to the callsite (2) and statement (3) is eliminated. The logic in the the generated code is: ? ? [P1] = callee(in1, in2, P(1, 1))  ?This optimization incorrectly changes the evaluation order of P(1, 1)  .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation for Reinitialize Function block?might produce different answers when OptimizeBlockOrder is set to speed

The Subsystem block that represents the parent subsystem has Show subsystem reinitialize ports ?selected. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might return incorrect output if a?dlarray?object is constructed from another dlarray object
Description: The generated code for the?dlarray  constructor might return an incorrectly sized dlarray  object if all  these?conditions are true: The original dlarray  object has only one non-singleton dimension. The original dlarray  object was previously permuted or  the data labels were not in 'SCBTU' order during construction. The newly constructed dlarray  object is labelled with a scalar format label.  For example, suppose that you use the?codegen  command to generate code for the function foo :function out = foo()a = dlarray(ones(2,1), 'TS');out = dlarray(a, 'S');end The generated code incorrectly outputs a 1(S) x 2 (U) shaped dlarray ?object. The expected output is a? 2(S) x 1(U) shaped?dlarray ?object.
Workaround: Before constructing a new?dlarray?object,?call the?extractdata function on the original dlarray?object.For example, modify the previous example function foo in this way:function out = foo()a = dlarray(ones(2,1), 'TS');out = dlarray(extractdata(a), 'S');end
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when reusable subsystem inlines a function call from referenced model
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code when all ?of these conditions are met:  The top model contains a Reusable Subsystem. The top model is configured with Code interface packaging  set to Nonreusable function .? The Reusable Subsystem outputs more than one function call.? Summary: View and Commit Changes?dialog does not open from the Source Control menu

Workaround: To avoid this issue, close the View and Commit Changes?dialog only by clicking Cancel in the dialog rather than the X in its banner.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect signal output for bus element port driven by Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block in rapid accelerator mode

Bus element port is driven by another block with a sample time different from the Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for dlarray method fullyconnect?might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for the  dlarray method  fullyconnect ?might produce incorrect results ?if both  of these conditions are true:  The name-value pair ' DataFormat' ?is used.  In the? ' DataFormat' ?value you pass to  fullyconnect , the label  'T'  appears before? 'B' . ?For example,? 'TBSC' ,  'TSBC' , or  'STCB' .

Summary: Selecting data in Create Signal live task might produce errors in MATLAB at Command Window
Description: In the?Create Signal ?live task, when you set?Output Format ?to?Vector ? and select points in the plot, this error might be shown in the MATLAB Command Window: Error using matlab.ui.internal.controller.uitable.utils.TableSelectionValidator.validateColumnSelectionSelection indices are out of data boundary.Error in matlab.ui.internal.controller.uitable.utils.TableSelectionValidator.validateCellSelection (line 108)????????????TableSelectionValidator.validateColumnSelection(storage, selection(:,2)');

Summary: Code mapping becomes unresolved because Embedded Coder dictionary is lost when saving by using the "Save model before closing?" dialog
Description: When you save a model by using the "Save model  before closing?"  dialog, the default Embedded Coder dictionary might be lost. Opening the model produces this warning: Warning: Model 'model ' has a C code mapping but has no Embedded Coder? Dictionary. Suggested Actions:  ? ? ? Create an Embedded Coder Dictionary in model 'model '. - Fix

Summary: MATLAB can crash during HDL Code Generation in the presence of feedback loops with RAM blocks.
Description: A crash can occur during HDL code generation without useful error messages when distributed pipelining is enabled for your model, if all of the following conditions are met: A feedback loop exists in the model. In this feedback loop, all sequential elements are RAM blocks.
Workaround: Add Delay blocks in the feedback loop so that the feedback loop contains sequential elements that are not RAM blocks.

Summary: Error during test generation analysis for models containing S-functions with boolean inputs or outputs
Description: Test generation analysis fails and throws following error when a model contains an S-function with boolean inputs or outputs: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end .

Summary: Velocity predicted by SGP4 orbit propagator in satellite scenario is incorrect
Description: When a satellite is assigned the SGP4 orbit propagator in a satellite scenario, the magnitude of the velocity prediction for this satellite might deviate by about 2 m/s, for example: >> sc = satelliteScenario;?  >> sat = satellite(sc,"eccentricOrbitSatellite.tle",OrbitPropagator="sgp4");?  >> [p,v] = states(sat,sc.StartTime);?  >> disp(v);
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report certain MCDC objectives from library C charts as Unsatisfiable
Description: When the MCDC objectives are from logical expressions in assignment statements in states or transitions of a library?C chart, Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly report them as Unsatisfiable.
Workaround: Before invoking Simulink Design Verifier, disable the library links from model to library C charts.

Summary: autosar.api.syncModel?converts some Simulink.Signals inside Stateflow charts to AUTOSAR.Signal objects in the base workspace
Description: After using?autosar.api.syncModel , some?Simulink.Signal  objects that are used to define data stores inside Stateflow charts might no longer appear in code mappings, for example to StaticMemory or ArTypedPerInstanceMemory. Instead they appear as?AUTOSAR.Signal ?objects in the base workspace.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: pagemldivide and pagemrdivide silently return an incorrect result for certain specially structured complex matrices
Description: Calling pagemldivide  or pagemrdivide  on a complex array containing one or more pages with certain special structure may return an incorrect result. To determine if an array has this special structure, call the function?BugReport2898758_InputChecker.m from the attached ZIP file with that array as input.
Workaround: The mldivide and mrdivide functions (\ and / operators) do not experience this bug. You can call these functions in a for-loop over the pages of the array as a workaround.

Summary: createDeploymentScript function is missing from an installation of the MATLAB Compiler product
Description: In an installation of MATLAB Compiler in R2022b, the createDeploymentScript function is missing.

Summary: Code Generation Advisor returns error about display during parallel build
Description: If you set the model configuration parameter Check model before generating code ?to On  for a referenced model during a parallel build, the build might terminate and return an error. Possible errors include: Support for Java user interfaces is required,  which is not available on this platform.?  Can't create explorer because there is no display connection.   The Code Generation Advisor does not display results of advisor checks.
Workaround: Set Check model before generating code to Off for all referenced models before running a parallel build. You can run checks for the referenced models by using the Code Generation Advisor dialog box.

Summary: Plot signal table for Signal Editor interface might show small time values as zero
Description: In the plot signal table for the Signal Editor interface, time values less than zero with data values less than .01 are rendered as zero.? For example, .004  is rendered incorrectly as zero.
Workaround: To correctly render the value, click the edit field for the table cell containing the value.

Summary: Signal Editor?Fit to view does not scale data in time direction if time is less than 1 second
Description: In the Signal Editor tool, signal data between 0 and 1 second does not fit in the view.
Workaround: To scale the data to the plot, zoom in and zoom out of the plot.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated S-Function uses incorrect SIMD intrinsic functions
Description: When you generate an S-Function from a subsystem, the generated S-Function might contain incorrect usage of vector intrinsic functions. This incorrect code generation occurs when these conditions are met: The model contains a subsystem You generate an S-Function from the subsystem by using?C/C++ code > Generate S-Function  The parameter Hardware Implementation > Hardware board  is set to Determine by Code Generation system target file   For the attached example, this generated S-Function generates incorrect code in Subsystem_sf.c : ? _mm_loadu_pd(((const real_T **)ssGetInputPortSignalPtrs(S,0))[i])?  The code is incorrect because? _mm_loadu_pd  loads data from incorrect memory locations instead of these expected locations: ?ssGetInputPortSignalPtrs(S,0))[i]),? ssGetInputPortSignalPtrs(S,0))[i+1]?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 5, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variable-size output signal might not be properly initialized in generated code or Rapid Accelerator
Description: A variable-size output signal, connected to a root outport block, might be incorrectly initialized in generated code or when simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode if the signal originates in a conditionally executed subsystem. This might happen if the output value of the conditionally executed subsystem is held and the execution of the subsystem is not triggered during the step function. In releases prior to R2011a, this bug also affects legacy products Stateflow Coder, Link for TASKING, Link for Code Composer Studio, and the various Embedded Targets and Target Support Packages.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Code generation halts with error:?Unrecognized function or variable rtNaN
Description: Code generation halts with error: Unrecognized function or variable rtNaN  This error indicates that a code generation failure occurred for the model construct where the output signal from a rate transition block splits and provides input into two or more different asynchronous function call subsystems.
Workaround: To resolve this code generation error, you can apply either of these solutions:Split the signal before the rate transition block. Use a rate transition for each signal that is input to the asynchronous function call subsystems.In Configuration Parameters > Solver > Solver details, enable Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer after removing the rate transition block(s) at the split(s).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: HDL code generation might fail for a model that contains?bus-element ports that are terminated
Description: This error happens whenever a Subsystem contains bus-element ports and all or some of the signals of the bus-element port are terminated and are not connected to any logic elements and Subsystem has a mask.? In this situation HDL Code generation may fail with an error messages: In the presence? of optimizations settings that require 'Delay Balancing ' you would see the following error:  ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Failed : For the block '<path_to_Block>' Delay balancing unsuccessful because Subsystem contains Unsupported blocks. Offending Block :<Block_name>.  In presences of no optimizations or optimizations that do not introduce additional latency that needs balancing you would see a model generation error  ? ? ? ? ? ? Errors occurred during model generation. Attempting to continue code generation. Invalid Simulink object name: <Path_to_Block>  ?HDL Coder is expected to ignore the terminated ports and continue to HDL generation in such scenario without these unexpected errors.?
Workaround: Consider replacing the "Bus-element port" with the "Bus-port followed by Bus-selector" until this issue is resolved in such modeling patterns.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Feedback loop with reference model block might cause HDL simulation mismatch due to latency introduced by optimizations upstream
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model might result in a numeric mismatch during simulation if the following conditions are met: Operations in a reference Simulink model involve performing arithmetic using a prior value from a feedback loop formed outside the reference model,  Latency is introduced from the upstream logic during code generation due to optimizations such as pipeline insertion, and resource sharing/streaming  HDL Coder does not properly schedule or reset this accumulation due to the latency introduced upstream. In the generated model, the feedback loop executes on the introduced cycles of latency, which can result in a numeric mismatch.
Workaround: Convert the reference model to subsystem will resolve the mismatch

Summary: Incorrect results for Simulink Test qualification test case tTIR when using report format .docx
Description: When the qualification summary report for Simulink Test is generated by using?qualkitdoCtrl.sltest.reportFormat= {'docx'} ,? the output for test case tTIR  is incorrectly marked as FAILED . This is a result of a false negative comparison between the expected and actual results.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when an enumeration block is defined in a System Object
Description: MATLAB crashes when an enumeration is defined in a System object and instantiated using the MATLAB command window. Enumerations are not supported. Consider the following System Object definition:?? classdef sampleSysObj < matlab.System   ? ?enumeration   ? ? ? red,yellow,green;  ? ?end   ? ?methods (Access = protected)  ? ? ? function y = stepImpl(obj,u)  ? ? ? ? ?% Implement algorithm. Calculate y as a function of input u and % discrete states.  ? ? ? ? ?y = u;  ? ? ? end  ? ?end  end ?  MATLAB crashes when the object is instantiated from the command window. >>? h = sampleSysObj

Summary: Polyspace Code Prover analysis of large applications in batch mode might not scale correctly
Description: Analysis with Polyspace Code Prover in batch mode (using?-batch  option) for an application with more than 30,000 lines of code might exhibit scaling issues. The same analysis run locally does not suffer scaling issues. You might also see small variations in the number of unproven checks for large applications run in batch mode compared to locally analyzed.
Workaround: Use the option -disable-native-pointers to revert to a pre-R2022b pointer analysis mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The code generator might produce unsafe concurrent code if a root inport is mapped to a receiver service with during-execution communication and drives multiple export functions or subsystems that have different rates
Description: The code generator might produce unsafe concurrent code when all ?of the following are true: The model contains a root inport that is mapped to a receiver service interface The receiver service interface uses the During execution ?communication method  The root inport drives multiple export functions or subsystems that have different rates   ?The generated code might access an internal buffer from multiple concurrent entry point functions. This creates race conditions in the generated code.
Workaround: No known workaround.

Summary: Simulating Simscape models in frequency-and-time mode or with Start simulation from steady state selected?might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: This issue manifests in: Frequency-and-time simulation of models that involve pure differential variables, that is, where a variable appears in the equations in differentiated form only Time simulation of frequency-and-time-compatible models with?Start simulation from steady state ?selected  If the solver encounters difficulty, it performs diagnostics, and this might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect if class?property is assigned a sparse value?and validated as the underlying type
Description: Generated code might incorrect if your MATLAB code satisfies?both ?these conditions: Your MATLAB class uses the property validation syntax?to set the type of a property to?double ?or logical  Elsewhere in your MATLAB code, this property is assigned from a sparse value with the same base type  In such situations, the generated code "casts" the assigned value to the property type (for example, a non-sparse?double ), instead of assigning the sparse value directly to the property. This issue affects MATLAB Coder and Simulink (MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks).

Description: Execution of these Simulink Coverage model coverage validation tests stops when Fixed-Point Designer is not available: certkitiec_slcov_sf.m  certkitiec_slcov_etc.m  certkitiec_slcov_dv.m   You will experience an error similar to: Beginning report??  Initializing test environment...??  Error : Unrecognized field name "Name".??  Validation report generation stopped. Follow the test execution procedure specified in IEC Certification Kit User Guide.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for a Delay block in the frame-based processing when Use Floating Point is enabled
Description: HDL code generation fails for a Delay block in frame-based processing when you use these settings: Input to the Delay block is not of floating-point type.? Use Floating Point  ?configuration parameter is enabled.??
