Summary: Model Advisor check?Identify blocks that generate expensive rounding code throws an?incorrect warning for Bit to Integer Converter?blocks
Description: Model Advisor check Identify blocks that generate expensive rounding code  (ID: mathworks.codegen.ExpensiveSaturationRoundingCode )? throws the following warning for Bit to Integer Converter  blocks. Warning The following blocks were found to contain a data type conversion with an expensive rounding mode. For each block, check the 'Integer rounding mode' setting and make sure it is set to one of the suggested modes.  ID:? test/Subsystem/Bit to Integer Converter1 Roundingmode:?Floor Suggested rounding mode:?Zero, Simplest? ? ? ? ? ? ? The recommended action in the warning message is incorrect as the?Bit to Integer Converter? block does not have a block parameter named?Integer rounding mode.

Summary: Alignment calculated incorrectly when using Tasking/Tricore compiler
Description: If you run an analysis on a project that is configured to use the Tasking  compiler with target Tricore , Polyspace might calculate the alignment of variables and structure fields incorrectly. The issue occurs when you use polyspace-configure  to extract the build configuration and pass the --eabi  option to the Tasking compiler. polyspace-configure  ignores this option.? For example, for this structure declaration:? typedef struct {  ? float myFloat;  ? char myChar;  } MyStruct;   The Tasking  compiler uses a half-word alignment by default, which means that the alignment requirement for MyStruct  is 2, leading to a size of 6. If you pass option --eabi=-half-word-align  to the compiler, the half-word alignment is disabled and the alignment requirement becomes 4. The size of MyStruct  then becomes 8. Because the disabling of the half-word alignment is ignored, if, for instance, you cast a pointer to myStruct  to pointer to a 32-bit or 64-bit int  such as in this code snippet:typedef unsigned long long int uint64_t;  char a;  MyStruct b;  volatile uint64_t *c = (volatile uint64_t*) &b;?   Polyspace reports a red Illegally Dereferenced Pointer (IDP) for the access to *c.
Workaround: A possible workaround for this issue is to pass option -compiler-parameter --no-half-word-align to Polyspace to disable half-word alignment.In the Polyspace graphical interface, you specify this option by going to Advanced Settings on the Configuration pane and entering the option in the Other field.?
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Specifying multiple CustomInclude, CustomSource, or CustomLibrary paths during code generation might produce an incorrect warning
Description: When generating code by using the MATLAB Coder App, if you specify multiple paths for the Additional include directories   (CustomInclude ), Additional source files  (CustomSource ), or?Additional libraries  (CustomLibrary ) settings, code generation might produce this warning in the build log:? Warning: Using whitespace or pathsep to delimit files for CustomSource,  CustomInclude,  and CustomLibrary is deprecated.  Todelimit files,  make each file name a separate element of a cell array or array of strings.  If specifying a single file with aspace in the path,  use a cell array of character vectors.   This warning might also occur at the command line if you run the codegen  command with the .prj  file as a parameter. In both these situations, this warning is incorrect and you can ignore it.  If you encounter this warning in workflows other than the two described above, the warning indicates a use of deprecated functionality. The warning should then be addressed for forward compatibility with future versions of MATLAB Coder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size struct field incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code when all ?of these are true: An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size struct field The right-hand side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size struct field using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the statement. The generated code modifies the size of the variable-size struct field prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This causes incorrect behavior when the read of the variable-size struct field also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. The generated code might read uninitialized memory or incorrect numeric results. The attached example ReproductionModel.zip  demonstrates this issue. This code snippet from the example shows the coding pattern described above: s.f1 = s.f1(2,2) - temp;   See this corresponding generated code: i = s->f1->size[0] * s->f1->size[1];  s->f1->size[0] = temp->size[0];  s->f1->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(sp, s->f1, i, &d_emlrtRTEI);  b_s = s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1];  for (i = 0; i <= loop_ub; i++) {  ? s->f1->data[i] = b_s - temp_data[i];  }   The expression? s->f1->data[s->f1->size[0] + 1] ?in the generated code?corresponds to the MATLAB expression? s.f1(2,2) . Note that the size of the struct field  s->f1  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. The generated code should update the size of  s->f1 ?after that expression.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 2, R2023b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: gradient?with two inputs might not apply a scalar spacing input correctly
Description: If?F  is not a vector and h  is a scalar, ?[dx,dy,...] = gradient(F,h)   only applies the spacing h  to the dx  output. Su bsequent outputs are computed with a spacing of 1 . MATLAB execution computes all outputs with the spacing h .
Workaround: Do any one of the following:?Apply the spacing manually:? [dx,dy,...] = gradient(F/h).Supply the scalar h?input for each dimension of F.? For example, if?F?is 3-D,??[dx,dy,dz] = gradient(F,h,h,h).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect if function input is a sparse value?and is validated as the underlying type
Description: Generated code might incorrect if your MATLAB code satisfies?both ?these conditions: One of your MATLAB functions uses an?arguments ?block to set the type of an input argument to?double ?or?logical  There is a call to this function whose corresponding argument is a sparse value with the same base type  In such situations, the generated code "casts" the input value to the?arguments ?type (for example, a non-sparse?double ), instead of using the sparse value directly. This issue affects MATLAB Coder and Simulink (MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks).

Summary: Generated code might contain MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation for a model that has Optimize global data access ?configuration parameter set to Minimize global data access .  The violation occurs because of the presence of dead code in the generated code.
Workaround: Set the configuration parameter Optimize global data access?to either None?or Use global to hold temporary results.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: writeall?function has low performance when writing to remote locations
Description: writeall(ds,outputLocation)  writes the data from the input datastore ds  to output files at the locationspecified in outputLocation . The number of output files is the same as thenumber of files referenced by the datastore. Performance can be slow because writeall  validates the?outputLocation  each time it generates a file name for each output file.

Summary: Getting error message during an OpenDRIVE import in the Driving Scenario Designer.?
Description: If an OpenDRIVE file contains multiple markings more than 30 with in a lane, and you import this file into the Driving Scenario Designer app, the app returns this error. ? Error message: Number of lane marking objects specified at the input must be less than 30.

Summary: Importing road network from ASAM OpenDRIVE file can show unrealistic elevation changes
Description: When you import a road network from an ASAM OpenDRIVE file into the Driving Scenario Designer  app or a drivingScenario  object, the road network can show unrealistic elevation changes. This issue occurs if the ASAM OpenDRIVE file specifies only one record each for geometry  and the elevation  elements of a road.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Internal assertion occurred during the HDL code generation when you use Selector block for selecting signal from Bus ports
Description: When your model containing a Selector block that is used to select or de-mux signal from bus ports by setting the Selector block parameter?Index Option ?to?Index vector (port)or Starting index (port) , HDL code generation fails with the following internal assertion error:? Assertion Failed With Debug Message: B:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ pir_transforms\ CoreCompLowering. hpp:90:  To select or de-mux signal from bus ports, use Bus Selector block.??
Workaround: Consider using Bus Selector block when selecting or de-muxing signal from bus ports.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inlined parameters inside subsystems with constant inports and "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences" setting enabled may use incorrect values
Description: Incorrect value may be used for inlined parameters in the code generated for models which meet all the following conditions: The parameter is defined using a Constant block that has the Sample Time  parameter set to 'Inf '. The Constant block is connected to an atomic subsystem and the subsystem inport gets constant sample time. "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences " setting is enabled for the atomic subsystem "Default parameter behavior " is set to "Inlined ".
Workaround: Change the sample time on the Constant block connected to atomic subsystem with "Minimize algebraic loop occurrences" enabled from 'Inf' (constant) to '-1' (inherited).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing in a for-loop for discontiguous signals
Description: The generated code for a Simulink block or S-function might use an incorrect for -loop index instead of producing an error when all of the following conditions are true: The block has a discontiguous input signal. The block has a tunable parameter that uses a field of a structure variable.
Workaround: Pass the block input signal through a Signal Conversion block that has the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter selected.If you are using an S-function with custom TLC code and Simulink R2023b or later, use the enhanced TLC interface.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder produces incorrect code when the inputs to?mldivide or mrdivide?function?are of different data types
Description: The mldivide  ("\ ") and?mrdivide  ("/ ") functions calculate the root of linear equations such?x = A\B and  x = B/A.  However, GPU coder might generate incorrect code for the?mldivide  or mrdivide ?function when all  of these conditions are true: Linear equation?Ax=B ?or  xA=B  does not have a solution Inputs A  and B  are of different data types

Summary: Using Function Caller block with a bus object defined in Simulink data dictionary for argument specifications causes an error
Description: In the Function Caller block dialog, if you use the name of a bus object defined in a Simulink data dictionary for the input or output argument specifications, you will get the following error because only the bus object defined in the base workspace is recognized:? Input argument specifications at Function Caller block'myFunctionCallerBlock' must specify examples of built-in datatypes. Examples of user-defined data types, including Bus, Fixed-point, Enumerations,and Alias types, may be provided with a Simulink.Parameter object.?
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Move the bus object to the MATLAB base workspace.Use Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct to create a full structure that corresponds to a bus and use the structure for the input and output argument specifications. This workflow is very similar to specifying bus data for a Constant block. For more information see?Using Bus Objects as the Output Data Type.

Summary: Code generation might fail for a model passing a Simulink.LookupTable object as a model argument
Description: Code generation might fail when a model passes a Simulink.LookupTable  object as a model argument. In this case, the code generator reports an error similar to this:?Code generation assertion 'orgCoderGroupName. empty()' failed in 'B:\ matlab\ src\ slcg_coderdi_impl\ server\ CoderDIServerImpl. cpp:552'?

Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Genomics Viewer opens blank
Description: After opening the Genomics Viewer  app, the app window appears blank.

Summary: Incorrect results when multiple PS-Simulink Converter blocks using different Apply affine conversion settings are attached to the same source
Description: The issue manifests when: Your model has two or more PS-Simulink Converter blocks?attached to the same source block These blocks use affine units (degC  or degF ) Different PS-Simulink Converter blocks use different?Apply affine ?conversion ?settings?  If all these conditions are true, one or more of the output signals from the PS-Simulink Converter blocks?might be incorrect.

Summary: Code coverage measurement can fail if the model contains variants
Description: Code coverage measurement?might fail to generate results in software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation mode if your model contains variants and you changed the active variant since the last time you ran a SIL or PIL simulation.

Summary: Loading a System Composer architecture model might show that it is edited
Description: Newly loading a System Composer architecture model linked to an external Simulink Data Dictionary (SLDD), might show that the model is edited. The model is shown as edited when there are missing or broken references to interfaces or interface elements on the data dictionary that are referenced by ports in the architecture model.

Summary: Incorrect texture rendered for a sim3d.Actor object
Description: If a .bmp  file is set as the texture of a sim3d.Actor, the simulation 3D environment renders the image with a white and black texture. The actor does not use the texture specified in the?.bmp? file.
Workaround: To use a texture from a .bmp file, convert the file to a .jpg, .png, or an 8-bit .bmp?file.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A14-1-1 in struct or union
Description: Polyspace incorrectly reports violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 A14-1-1 on struct  or union  objects that contain the expected call to static_assert .

Summary: Crash during ASAP2 file export from Simulink
Description: When exporting ASAP2 files from Simulink, the code generation process might crash and display the following line in the initial stack frame: coder::descriptor::StructExpression::Impl::getBaseVariable?

Summary: Docking the MATLAB Editor, Live Editor, or Help Browser on macOS 13 (Ventura) causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When running MATLAB on macOS 13 Ventura, undocking and then redocking the MATLAB Editor, Live Editor, or Help Browser causes MATLAB to crash. This is an issue with MATLAB running through Rosetta on Apple silicon machines (M1 chips) and on Intel Macs.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor shows error for Product block configured as '/*' even when constraints are respected.
Description: Prior to R2023a, if you have a model that contains a Product block configured as '/*' that has inputs that are fixed-point data types, HDL Workflow Advisor incorrectly reports an error of incompatible types between the divisor and the dividend. This error occurs because the check for the fixed-point inputs to the Product block always takes the first signal as the dividend and the second signal as the divisor. If you set the order of the sign to '/*', the first signal becomes the divisor and the second signal becomes the dividend but the Product block continues to treat the first signal as the dividend and the second signal as the divisor.? In R2023a, the check for fraction length requirement based on the divisor or dividend inputs of the Product block now takes into account of the order of operator being used.

Summary: Variants are applied in incorrect order in Group Simulation or Fit Data programs
Description: The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app sometimes applies variants in the incorrect order when you run the Group Simulation  or Fit Data  programs. The incorrect order might be used when you run the program after making a change to the variants, such as unchecking the box to use a baseline variant. The order of variants might also differ when running the program versus viewing the program code. If you have fewer than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, then variants are in the correct order when you view the program code even if they are not applied in the correct order when running the program. If you have ten or more variant columns, then the variants will never be in the correct order when you view the program code. Note that the order of variants matters only if multiple variants modify the same quantity. In such cases, the last value is taken from the last variant to modify the component.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, update the variants to remove any overlapping content. Then, the results do not depend on the order in which variants are applied.If it is not practical to remove overlapping content, you can ensure correct results when running?a program by saving, reloading, and then rerunning a program after making any changes to the configuration of variants in that program. Reloading the project avoids the issue by updating the variant information stored in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app. However, if you have more than ten variant columns in the variant and dose setup table, variants are still applied in the incorrect order when viewing the program code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect access of variables that?use the?coder::array?template?with Dynamic Memory Allocation for Fixed-Size Arrays (DynamicMemoryAllocationForFixedSizeArrays) enabled
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code from your MATLAB code that meets all   of these conditions: Your MATLAB code uses the reshape  function. Dynamic Memory Allocation for Fixed-Size Arrays  (DynamicMemoryAllocationForFixedSizeArrays ) is enabled.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: function [IA6] = set0001_funToCall(IA4,IA6)? ? IA6{1} = [IA6{1},...? ? min(+IA4(4:1:20,8:1:13),...? ? bitand(IA4(1:1:17,2:3:17),reshape(repmat(IA6{1}(4,3),102,1),[17,6])))];end  The code generated from the MATLAB function?set0001_funToCall ?is shown below:  ?coder::array<short, 2U> r2;  ? coder::array<short, 1U> r1;    r2 = r1. reshape(17,  6);?? ? coder::internal::minimum2(b_IA4, r, r2);?  ? r1[0][0]; The generated code writes the result of the reshape ?function to a temporary variable r2, ?which performs a deep copy of r1 . The subsequent calling coder::internal::minimum2 ?operation on the temporary variable r2  is meant to modify shared memory between r1  and r2 ?but does not occur because of the previous deep copy of r1 . Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent changes to the IANA time zone database, including time zones in Mexico
Description: MATLAB uses the IANA time zones database for up-to-date time zone information. MATLAB is missing recent updates to this database. As of the R2022b general release, MATLAB includes database version 2022a. However, in R2022b MATLAB does not include the 2022f version of the IANA database, which also captures time zone updates from database versions 2022b, 2022c, 2022d, and 2022e. Most notably, these updates include changes to a number of time zones which no longer observe daylight saving time (DST). For example, in October 2022 the government of Mexico announced that the country no longer observes DST except near the US border.?The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. IANA Time Zone Database:?https://www.iana.org/time-zones  In versions of MATLAB that do not have these changes, datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period will have incorrect UTC offsets. Specifically, they will be one hour ahead of the actual times in those time zones.
Workaround: If possible, update to a release with an IANA time zone database version of 2022f or later.To check the IANA time zone database version in MATLAB, use the second output of the timezones function. When the database is up to date, the second output is '2022f'.>> [~,vers] = timezones vers =    '2022f'https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/ref/timezones.htmlIf you are unable to update to a newer release, you can obtain the correct time zone information for datetime arrays on or after October 30th 2022 (where the desired time zone is in Mexico) by using?the corresponding fixed time zone offset. For example, replace the time zone 'America/Chihuahua'?with the time zone 'UTC-6' on or after?October 30th, 2022.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

This issue affects MATLAB Coder and Simulink (MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks).??

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using the editor with tab completion and import statements
Description: While using the MATLAB editor with tab completion enabled (i.e. "Show suggestions automatically" is checked for the MATLAB Editor Automatic Completions Preferences) and import statements, MATLAB may crash when saving the file. The crash happens when the file contains expressions that end in dot (e.g. "apgtl.EventType.") or use dynamic expressions to access members of a class (e.g. "apgtl.EventType.(dynamicExpression)"). For example, the following function with import statements and a dynamic expression with a class enumeration member causes a crash when it is saved: function myFunction(enumName)  import matlab.alias.AliasDefinition;  table(apgtl.EventType.(enumName))  end  Note that "apgtl.EventType" is a MATLAB enumeration class defined within a namespace called "apgtl". The crash happens when MATLAB attempts to tab complete the line "apgtl.EventType.(enumName)". The stack trace in the crash log? contains references to mcos_impl, m_dispatcher and tabcompletion: ?[? 0] 0x00007ffd131c8fa4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+03575716 [? 1] 0x00007ffd132ded5f C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04713823 [? 2] 0x00007ffd132d18c3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcos_impl.dll+04659395 [? 3] 0x00007ffd241ae7be C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\m_dispatcher.dll+00845758 [? 4] 0x00007ffd241b0a37 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\m_dispatcher.dll+00854583 [? 5] 0x00007ffd0ae2e3dd C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\tabcompletion.dll+00648157
Workaround: The crash can be avoided by doing any of the following:Remove any MATLAB expressions that end in dot or use dynamic expressions to access members of a class.Omit import statements.Turning off tab completion. Go to the MATLAB toolstrip Home tab and click on Preferences,? Editor/ Debugger, Automatic Completions and uncheck "Show suggestions automatically".

Summary: fillmissing incorrectly fills non-NaN MissingLocations for movmean or movmedian methods
Description: fillmissing  computes incorrect fill values if all of the following conditions are met:  The fill method is movmean   or movmedian  The MissingLocations   name-value argument is used and the specified locations do not correspond to NaN  values Only one output is requested   For example:>> F = fillmissing([1 2 5], "movmedian",3, MissingLocations=[false true false]) gives the result:   [1?????2?????5] instead of the correct result: [1?????3?????5]
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by requesting a second output from fillmissing:>> [F,~] = fillmissing([1 2 5],"movmedian",3,MissingLocations=[false true false])gives the correct result: [1?????3?????5]

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing DDS struct field setter when the output of a Bus Creator block is connected to a Write DDS Sample block
Description: The DDS struct field setter might be missing from the generated code for a DDS application when all of the following conditions are true: A Bus Creator block is used to generate a DDS Struct type The output of the Bus Creator block is directly connected to a Write DDS Sample block
Workaround: Place the Bus Creator block inside a subsystem.Configure that subsystem to be atomic.On the Code Generation pane of the subsystem block parameters, select "Reusable function".Connect the output of that subsystem to the Write DDS Sample?block.

Summary: createInputDataset function might generate results that do not match model interface
Description: The?createInputDataset ?function might output incorrect values when one or both of the following are true for a model:  A single bus element port represents the entire bus. Duplicate bus element ports exist.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Dashboard and Process Advisor generate warning when MATLAB code calls Polyspace function
Description: If there are calls to Polyspace functions in any MATLAB code, Process Advisor  and the dashboards generate the following warning while analyzing the code: Artifact <path to M or P file> is stored outside the project root.?The relationship? between <path to calling file> and this artifact will not be considered.  Note that the Process Advisor  app is available with the support package?CI/CD Automation for Simulink Check.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Building a model configured for row-major code generation that contains a Discrete FIR Filter, Discrete Filter, or Discrete Transfer Fcn block that is configured as a state owner block might generate incorrect code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code when all ?of these conditions are met: The model is configured to generate row-major code The model uses a discrete block such as a Discrete FIR block, a Discrete Filter block, or a Discrete Transfer Fcn block You configure the discrete block to be a state owner block by using a State Reader block and a State Writer block The discrete block has an input signal that is a vector or matrix The discrete block uses channel-based input processing
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Error when simulating Curve block inside model reference
Description: When you simulate a top model containing a Curve block inside a Model block, Simulink might throw an error similar to: Error in 'myComponent/Curve_Using_Map_Using_Prelookup_single/Map_single/Map1': Failed to evaluate mask initialization commands.  Caused by:  An error occurred during simulation of Model block 'myComponent_top/Model'.?  Cannot change parameter 'Output data type: (OutDataTypeStr)' of 'myComponent/Curve_Using_Map_Using_Prelookup_single/Map_single/Map1' while simulation is running?

Summary: Models might simulate slowly, stop responding, or produce a wrong answer if the Minimize algebraic loop?occurrences?parameter is enabled for referenced model
Description: Simulations of a model hierarchy might run slowly, stop responding, or produce a wrong answer due to an incorrect Jacobian pattern when all of these conditions are met: The model uses an implicit solver with the Solver Jacobian method ?parameter set to Sparse perturbation .  The model contains one or more referenced models that are part of an algebraic loop. One or more referenced models in an algebraic loop has the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences ?parameter enabled.?
Workaround: To avoid or resolve this issue, use the full perturbation solver Jacobian method in the top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.Select the Solver?pane.?Expand Solver details.Set the Solver Jacobian method?to Full perturbation.For some models, the full perturbation solver Jacobian method might not be an appropriate choice or might slow simulation. If using the full perturbation solver Jacobian method does not work for your model, you can disable algebraic loop minimization for the referenced models instead.To disable the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences?parameter for a referenced model:Open the referenced model as a top model.In the Simulink Toolstrip, on the Modeling?tab, click Model Settings.?Select the Model Referencing?tab.Under Options for referencing this model?clear Minimize algebraic loop occurrences.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Microsoft Excel date values stored in the 1904 date system as doubles might be incorrectly imported by readcell
Description: readcell  imports datenum  values using the 1900 date system. In releases R2019a through R2021a, when UseExcel  is true  and DatetimeType  is exceldatenum , returned values originally stored using the 1904 date system are 1462 days greater than expected. For more information on the 1900 and 1904 date systems, see?Date Systems in Excel .
Workaround: In releases R2019a through R2021a, to read the correct datenum values, specify the name-value argument UseExcel as false.>> C = readcell(filename,"DatetimeType","exceldatenum","UseExcel",false)If unspecified, the default value for UseExcel is false.>> C = readcell(filename,"DatetimeType","exceldatenum")
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: HDL code generation errors out for crp_temp block which does not exist in original model
Description: When using clock-rate pipelining on a multi-rate model, this delay balancing error might be encountered that points to a block that does not exist in the original model: Delay balancing unsuccessful because different rates across the block cannot be handled by delay balancing. Consider making it single-rate.  Offending Block: crp_temp

Summary: When comparing text files,?the Comparison Tool might use the wrong comparison type?and display an invalid Simulink model error
Description: The Comparison Tool might use the wrong comparison?type when you compare text files. The comparison report displays:?File '...' does not contain a valid Simulink model  This issue does not apply to workflows that use mlDiff and mlMerge to open the Comparison Tool.
Workaround: When you compare text files, specify the comparison type.Command Window:>> visdiff(firstFilePath,secondFilePath,'text')?On the Home, Editor, or?Live Editor?tabs, in the?File?section, click?Compare.?Set the Comparison type field to "Text comparison".

Summary: Passing string literals to external C functions in a C Function block might crash MATLAB
Description: Simulating a model containing C Function blocks may crash MATLAB or generate incorrect results when the following conditions are true: There is a custom code function call within a C Function block. The function call passes a string literal to the input argument of char ? type, for example:?my_function(u, "my text");
Workaround: Assign string literal to a local char array variable. Pass the char?array to the external C function, for example;const char str[] = "my text";?my_function(u, str);

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may report a model as incompatible if any referenced model has the diagnostics option?Automatic solver parameter selection?set to error
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may report a model as incompatible if any of its referenced models have the Diagnostics option?Automatic solver parameter selection ? set to?error . An example error message: Unable to determine a fixed step size based on the sample times in the model 'ReferencedModel', because the model does not have any discrete sample times. You can disable this diagnostic by explicitly specifying a fixed step size in the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, or setting the 'Automatic solver parameter selection' diagnostic to 'none' in the Solver group on the Diagnostics pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: On all referenced models, set?Automatic solver parameter selection to?warning or none,?before invoking Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A3-1-5 on constexpr function members
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises violations of AUTOSAR-C++14 A3-1-5 on constexpr  function members defined in class declarations, whereas they need to be known at compilation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result when a referenced model that accesses a global data store is simulated in accelerator mode
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy might produce incorrect results when all these conditions apply: A referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. That referenced model accesses a global data store defined by a Simulink.Signal  object in a data dictionary or the base workspace. The Initial value ?property of that?Simulink.Signal  object is set to a MATLAB variable or Simulink.Parameter  object. That MATLAB variable or Simulink.Parameter  object is not used elsewhere in the referenced model.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Unable to save Simulink models, Live Scripts, MLAPP files, App Designer apps, SimBiology projects, or preferences on Linux
Description: Attempting to save Simulink models, Live Scripts, MLAPP files, SimBiology projects or App Designer apps results in the error: Invalid cross-device link (18) . This error occurs across Linux distributions Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS, Fedora 36, etc. Also impacted are MATLAB preferences and settings. For example, parallel cluster, font, and color settings do not persist after closing and restarting MATLAB.? The issue is not seen on Windows, Mac, or MATLAB Online.
Workaround: Possible workarounds include the following:Invalid cross-device link (18) when saving a file on arch LinuxMATLAB preferences not being saved across sessionsWhy aren't my MATLAB preferences saved on Linux?

Summary: Modbus read and write functions error or return incorrect values
Description: When the Modbus read  or write  functions are called simultaneously in callback functions and in the main MATLAB code, they might error or return incorrect values.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point QR Givens rotation in MATLAB produces wrong answer when the input has a pivot element that is purely imaginary (imaginary part is non-zero, real part is zero)
Description: These functions use the fixed-point QR Givens Rotations and are impacted by this bug:? fixed.qrAB  fixed.qlessQR  fixed.cordicDivide  fixed.svd    This bug affects both MATLAB execution and code generation. The corresponding Simulink blocks are not impacted.? Follow these steps to reproduce the bug: Create A  with purely imaginary numbers (imaginary part is non-zero, real part is zero).  >> A=[1i,2i;3i,4i];   Create B  to be the identity matrix with dimension the same as size(A,1) .  >> B=[1,0;0,1];   Compute the QR decomposition with two inputs.  >> [C,R]=fixed.qrAB(A,B);   In fixed.qrAB , if the input A  is non-singular and the input B  is the identity matrix, then output C  corresponds to Q' from the QR decomposition and R  corresponds to the R from the QR decomposition.  The bug causes Q  to be singular (it should be unitary).  >> Q=C'    Q =  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 0.4472i  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 0.8944i   Additionally, R  is singular and A  is not singular.  >> R    R =  ? ? ? ? ?0? ? ? ? ?0  ? ? ? ? ?0? ? 4.4721   Q*R  should reproduce A , but Q*R  produces a matrix that has all zeros in the first column, when the first column should be [1i; 3i] .  >> Q*R    ans =  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 2.0000i  ? ?0.0000 + 0.0000i? ?0.0000 + 4.0000i    >> A    A =  ? ?0.0000 + 1.0000i? ?0.0000 + 2.0000i  ? ?0.0000 + 3.0000i? ?0.0000 + 4.0000i   And the difference between Q*R  and A  is large.  >> Q*R-A    ans =  ? ?0.0000 - 1.0000i? ?0.0000 - 0.0000i  ? ?0.0000 - 3.0000i? ?0.0000 - 0.0000i

Summary: Excessive model compile time when certain Simscape blocks are present
Description: When a model contains certain Simscape blocks, the compile time after clicking Run  can be very long. The issue stems from the index reduction algorithms taking an excessively long time. The affected blocks include (but are not limited to): System-Level Refrigeration Cycle (2P) System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) System-Level Condenser Evaporator (2P-TL) System-Level Heat Exchanger (2P-2P) System-Level Heat Exchanger (TL-MA)
Workaround: Turn off index reduction in affected models by opening the Solver Configuration block and setting Index reduction method to None.

Summary: Assertion failure when generating code for models containing Data Store Memory blocks and Simulink Function blocks or Stateflow charts
Description: Simulink might throw an assertion error when you generate code for a model where all of the following conditions are true: The model uses a Data Store Memory block to define a shared data store. A Data Store Read or Data Store Write block that accesses the data store is connected to the input or the output of either of these blocks: Simulink Function block. Stateflow chart that has the?Export Chart Level Function ?property selected.   The error assertion is similar to the following: Code generation assertion 'blkModRef && (*blkModRef).size() == 1'

Summary: Server crashes when HTTP client request has missing path or unknown HTTP verb with missing path
Description: In HTTP client requests to a MATLAB Production Serverinstance, if the request line has a missing path or an unknown HTTP verb with a missing path,then server crashes. For example, these request lines crash the server: GET HTTP/1.1  BADVERB HTTP/1.1  curl -v --url http://localhost:9910 --request-targetempty-path  This issue can occur, for example, when vulnerability scanners intentionally send bad requests to detect security flaws.?

Summary: Standalone Application installer fails to detect the existing MATLAB Runtime in the silent mode
Description: The Standalone Application installer generated by Application Compiler  app or?compiler.package.installer? fails to detect the existing MATLAB Runtime in the silent mode on Windows. As a result, another MATLAB Runtime will be installed. If using default installation path, the new MATLAB Runtime will be installed in?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Runtime\R2022b\R2022b, resulting in a nested folder.
Workaround: Uninstall the existing MATLAB Runtime first before running the installer.Or?Run the installer in silent mode with inputFile specifying the MATLAB Runtime installation destination folder to overwrite the existing install. The Installer_input.txt file, for example, can have below content and the runtime will be installed to C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Runtime\R2022bdestinationFolder=C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB RuntimeagreeToLicense=yesdesktopShortcut=truestartMenuShortcut=true

Summary: HDL code generation may fail for a?Unit Delay block placed inside?Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and optimizations are requested
Description: For your model containing a Unit Delay block that is placed inside the Resettable Synchronous Subsystem?and certain optimizations are applied on the model, HDL code generation fails with a following error message:? '<Path to Block>/Unit Delay/State Control' specifies classic semantics. It cannot be placed inside a subsystem that uses synchronous semantics. Use State Control blocks with the same settings for the 'State control' parameter.

Summary: Unrecognized method, property, or field?error during SIL/PIL simulation
Description: In a model that logs a signal, if the logged signal is a non-virtual bus and its implementation resolves to a storage class, then running a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation?can produce this error:? Unrecognized method,  property,  or field 'Identifier' for class 'coder. descriptor.BasicAccessFunctionExpression'
Workaround: Identify the logged signal that is causing the issue.Connect the signal to a Signal Copy block.Set the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?block parameter to on.Log the output of the Signal Copy block, which forces the creation of a variable in the generated code.

Summary: Software architecture model keeps showing that referenced software architecture model has unsaved changes
Description: Compiling a software architecture model, which contains a component that references another software architecture model, might mark the referenced model dirty if it does not contain any behavior components.
Workaround: Add a behavior component to the referenced software architecture model.

Summary: Expired download link for?Hamlin Beach dataset (rit18_data.mat)
Description: The download url (http://www.cis.rit.edu/~rmk6217/rit18_data.mat)?used in the?downloadHamlinBeachMSIData  helper function is no longer valid. This helper function is used in these examples that download and use the?rit18_data.mat ?data set: Semantic Segmentation of Multispectral Images Using Deep Learning (Image Processing Toolbox, Computer Vision Toolbox, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Application on ARM Neon Targets That Uses U-Net (MATLAB Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Application on Intel CPUs That Uses U-Net (MATLAB Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Network That Uses U-net (GPU Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Semantic Segmentation of Multispectral Images by Using Quantized U-Net on FPGA (Deep Learning HDL Toolbox)   Running any of these examples produces an error message like this: Error using load  Unable to find file or directory 'C:\Users\Username\AppData\Local\Temp\rit18_data\rit18_data.mat'.
Workaround: Before running these examples, replace the download link in the?downloadHamlinBeachMSIData?helper function with this link:url = "https://home.cis.rit.edu/~cnspci/other/data/rit18_data.mat";

Summary: Expired download link for?Hamlin Beach dataset (rit18_data.mat)
Description: The download url (http://www.cis.rit.edu/~rmk6217/rit18_data.mat)?used in the?downloadHamlinBeachMSIData ?helper function is no longer valid. This helper function is used in these examples that download and use the?rit18_data.mat ?data set: Semantic Segmentation of Multispectral Images Using Deep Learning (Image Processing Toolbox, Computer Vision Toolbox, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Application on ARM Neon Targets That Uses U-Net (MATLAB Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Application on Intel CPUs That Uses U-Net (MATLAB Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Code Generation for Semantic Segmentation Network That Uses U-net (GPU Coder, Deep Learning Toolbox) Semantic Segmentation of Multispectral Images by Using Quantized U-Net on FPGA (Deep Learning HDL Toolbox)  Running any of these examples produces an error message like this: Error using load  Unable to find file or directory 'C:\Users\Username\AppData\Local\Temp\rit18_data\rit18_data.mat'.
Workaround: Before running these examples, replace the download link in the?downloadHamlinBeachMSIData?helper function with this link:url = "https://home.cis.rit.edu/~cnspci/other/data/rit18_data.mat";

Summary: Calling step method of a System Object in an if?statement and assigning the output to variables that are unused causes error
Description: The following use of?step ?method in an?if ?statement can cause an error during HDL code generation because of the unused output variable, unused , of ?the step  method call . The  error message is? Assertion failed: !m_phiStmts.empty(). function y = fcn(data, address, enable)  persistent obj  if isempty(obj)  ? ? obj =? hdl.RAM;  end?  y = data;  if data > 0?  ? ?unused = step(obj, data, address, enable);?  end  To fix the error during HDL code generation, do not leave the output variables of step  method call unused.

Summary: False MISSING_MAKE_SHARED defect on the use of operator new[] for C++ versions older than C++20
Description: The overload make_shared<T>(N)  is not available for C++ version older than C++20. With earlier versions of C++, using the operator new[]  with std::make_shared  is not a performance defect.  For instance, the following falsely reports a defect: auto sp?= std::shared_ptr<char>(new char[N]); //False Positive for versions before C++20
Workaround: If possible, upgrade to compilers that support C++20.

Summary: HDL Coder can generate incorrect code from a MATLAB Function block that uses?hdl.treesum or hdl.treeprod to reduce a fixed-point matrix to a scalar
Description: When you generate HDL code using either of these options: A MATLAB Function block that has the HDL block property Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath  A MATLAB function with the Aggressive Dataflow Conversion  parameter enabled in Optimizations ?tab of the HDL Code Generation  task in the MATLAB HDL Workflow Advisor  either of these commands, if?X? is a fixed-point matrix, can result in incorrect code: hdl.treesum(X, 'all')  hdl.treeprod(X, 'all')

Summary: Incorrect frame origin location in the File Solid block when using Based on Geometric Feature
Description: In the File Solid block, when creating anew frame on a surface by selecting the Based on Geometric Feature ?check box, the frame must be located at thecentroid of the selected surface. However, for certain imported geometries, the created frame is located at an incorrect location.?

Summary: dicomwrite creates a corrupted file when an attribute contains more than 65534 elements
Description: When the dicomwrite  function writes a DICOM attribute whose value contains more than 65534 elements and whose VR (value representation) is one of the following values, the output file can be corrupted: 'AE', 'AS', 'AT', 'CS', 'DA', 'DS', 'DT', 'FD', 'FL', 'IS', 'LO', 'LT', 'PN', 'SH', 'SL', 'SS', 'ST', 'TM', 'UI', 'UL', 'US', 'UC', 'UR'  If you attempt to read data beyond this corrupted attribute, the read operation can error, return only a partial set of read data with a warning message, or return erroneous values.  For example, the dicominfo  function can return a partial set of read data with a warning message similar to the one below: Warning: Not enough data imported.? Attempted to read 926036316 bytes at position 66627.? Only read840897.

Summary: App help text is removed when loading an app that has code containing the less than sign surrounded with whitespace
Description: When loading an app that contains any code or comments with the less than sign surrounded with whitespace (e.g. " < "), other than with the class definition on line 1, any app help text previously specified for the app will be removed from the generated code in App Designer. If edits to the app are then saved, the former app help text will be permanently lost.
Workaround: The issue can be prevented from occurring by removing any whitespace surrounding any use of the less than sign: "<".If the app already contains code or comments with the less than sign and surrounding whitespace, first get a copy of the current help text by executing >> help MyApp.mlapp where MyApp.mlapp is the name of the app. Second, load the app, remove any whitespace surrounding all uses of the less than sign, and re-add the app help text copied from the first step.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing initial value of a chart output and recompiling can result in wrong answers
Description: Stateflow might retain incorrect type information from a previous model update or simulation when all of the following conditions are true: The output data of the chart is set to "inherit" The initial value of that output data is specified to a value other than the default  For example, if the initial value for the output is a 2x2 matrix of type double, and after running the simulation you change the initial value to a 4x4 matrix of type single, the output signal might retain its original size and type on subsequent runs, leading to wrong simulation results.?
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when requesting data from a Haver Analytics database
Description: When using the functions nextinfo  and info  to request variable information from a Haver Analytics database, MATLAB crashes with a message similar to the following: Assertion in struct mxArray_tag *__cdecl `anonymous-namespace'::MxCreateStringNCharsT_safe::operator ()(const char *,const char *,unsigned __int64) const at B:\matlab\foundation\matrix\src\matrix\array2.cpp line 500:mxCreateString called with non-UTF-8 input

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder produces incorrect code when a coder.ceval call modifies the same variable on CPU and GPU
Description: GPU Coder does not support coder.ceval  calls that concurrently modify the same variable on both CPU and GPU. An example of such a call is shown below: % X is a numeric arraycoder.ceval('cevalFunction', coder.ref(X), coder.ref(X,'gpu'));  However, GPU Coder does not raise an error when such a call exists and instead allows the code to be generated successfully. In such a case, the generated code might produce incorrect results depending on how the function called using coder.ceval  ('cevalFunction'  in the above example) is implemented.

Summary: Incorrect bistatic radar cross section (RCS) calculation using rcs function
Description: The values for the radar cross section (RCS) calculated under the bistatic configuration are incorrect. The bistatic configuration for the RCS calculation is achieved by specifying a single transmit angle, and one or more receive angles as input arguments to the rcs  function.  The monostatic configuration is unaffected by this issue.

Summary: ROS node generation fails for Simulink Models using Schedule Editor
Description: ROS node generation fails when the Simulink model contains Schedule Editor based partitions. Models with atomic subsystems or referenced models using Schedule Editor fail to generate ROS node.??

Summary: Possible MATLAB crash when simulating modally reduced flexible beams
Description: MATLAB might crash when simulating and visualizing a Simscape Multibody model that contains a flexible beam block configured to be modally reduced with a nonzero number of retained modes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can disable graphical display of modally reduced flexible beam blocks by selecting None?for their?Graphic >?Type?parameter.One possible strategy is to enable display for the beam but specify its Number of Retained Modes parameter to 0 while building the model. Later, when the model is ready for simulation, you can disable the beam's graphics and specify a non-zero number of retained modes to capture flexible behavior.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Block priorities are not honored inside variant choices of variant subsystems
Description: Blocks might not execute in an order determined by their block priorities when either of these conditions apply:The block priorities are specified for blocks inside a variant choice of a variant subsystem. The block priorities are specified for blocks that represent the variant choices of a variant subsystem and blocks inside these variant choices.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 7, R2023a

Summary: Connections to certain IP cameras fail when using an ipcam object
Description: After you install MATLAB Support Package for IP Cameras, the ipcam  object creation fails to establish a connection to certain IP cameras. Even though the user credentials of the IP camera are correct, the following error message appears:   "Unable to connect to the IP Camera Stream URL due to incorrect Username or Password."

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Structure variables in data dictionaries cannot be tuned in?Simulink Compiler
Description: In Simulink Compiler, tuned values of structure variables in data dictionaries are ignored without warning.?? Consider the following Simulink Compiler script. This script attempts to tune the field x  of the structure variable p . If p  is in a data dictionary, the simulation in the script does not use the tuned value of p.x . Also, the simulation does not issue a diagnostic indicating that the tuned value was ignored.? model = 'exampleModel';?  simInput = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);?  simInput = simInput.setVariable('p.x', 2);?  simInput = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInput);?  sim(simInput); ?
Workaround: If a structure variable in a data dictionary must be tuned, consider moving the variable to the base workspace (structure variables in the base workspace can be tuned in Simulink Compiler).?Non-structure variables can be tuned in Simulink Compiler whether or not they are in data dictionaries. If only a few fields of a structure variable need to be tuned, consider turning those fields into standalone variables.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated when mapping a sender service with the During Execution data communication method to a root level outport that is connected to nested For Each subsystem blocks
Description: When you map a sender service with the During Execution  data communication method to a root level outport that is connected to nested For Each subsystems, the generated code might incorrectly localize and optimize away the outport buffer in the subsystem calls. This leaves the buffer outdated before it sends information out through the service interface.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for AUTOSAR Adaptive model might lead to data race condition
Description: The generated code for an AUTOSAR Adaptive model might result in a data race condition if all the following conditions are true.? The model contains a provider port that provides an implementation to an adaptive method. The model contains?a Simulink Function block that provides a call through a function port. The function port is mapped to the adaptive method.  Under these conditions, the generated code initializes the provider port with MethodCallProcessingMode  as kEvent  instead of kEventSingleThread , which can lead to a data race if the method implementation does not synchronize data access.

Summary: Error when bus elements on inputs or outputs of design are single or half data type
Description: When bus elements on the inputs or outputs of a DUT are of type single or half, HDL Coder throws the following error message:?Failed Unrecognized method, property, or field 'isVector' for class 'hdlturnkey.data.TypeSingle'.
Workaround: For a workaround, use one of these options:Type cast the inputs to a fixed-point number outside the design and then convert the inputs back to single or half type inside the design.?Move the single or half type inputs outside of the bus.

Summary: Checkbox "Add a shortcut tothe desktop"?is missing in the Standalone Applicationinstaller UI
Description: The checkbox "Add a shortcut to the desktop "? is missing in the Standalone Applicationinstaller created by the Application Compiler  appor?the compiler.package.installer  function.
Workaround: You can manually create the desktop shortcut by going to the installed application folder (e.g. C:\Program Files\ApplicationName\application), right-clicking on the application (e.g. ApplicationName.exe), and selecting "Send to" and "Desktop (create shortcut)".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing depth-wise convolution with dilation values might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network that contains depth-wise convolution with non-unity dilation factor might produce incorrect results. This issue affects the ARM Mali target.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect accelerator mode simulation results when sample time independent model has initialize, reset, or terminate ports but no data ports
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy produces incorrect simulation results when all of the following conditions are met: A referenced model simulates in accelerator mode. That referenced model is a sample time independent model. That referenced model contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. That referenced model has no root-level input or output ports. The corresponding Model block has the?Show model initialize port ?and Schedule rates ?block parameters selected.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Internal error occurs during HDL code generation for?Data Type Conversion block with input as single-precision floating-point type and output is fixed-point type
Description: HDL code generation fails for a Simulink model that has Data Type Conversion (DTC) block and the following settings are applied: The input to DTC block is?single-precision floating-point type? and output is fixed-point  type Model configuration setting?Floating Point IP Library  is set to? Native Floating Point ? ?

Summary: Recommended Latency Accept button might not work in Dataflow Multicore Analysis report
Description: When the Dataflow Multicore Analysis report recommends changing the latency of the Dataflow subsystem to improve multicore performance an Accept  button is shown to set the latency to the recommended value and rerun the analysis. Clicking the button might have no effect so that the latency of the Dataflow subsystem does not change and the analysis is not rerun. When this happens the report still recommends a Latency and the number thread does not change.?
Workaround: Manually set the Dataflow subsystem latency and rerun the analysis.?Open the Property Inspector with the Dataflow subsystem selected.Go to the Execution tab in the Property Inspector.Set the Latency parameter in the Attributes section to the recommended value.Click Run Analysis in the Dataflow Multicore Tab toolstrip to rerun the analysis with the new latency.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root inputs or outputs of large,nested bus with Auto or ExportedGlobal storage class generate incorrectmemset statement in model initialization function
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect memset ?statement in the model initialization function when all ?of the following are true:  You generate code for a model that contains a nested bus that is 2560 bytes or larger. The bus uses the Auto ?or  ExportedGlobal ?storage class.  The bus includes fields of integer type.   The code generator might incorrectly use a memset ?statement in the model initialization function which causes nested properties to not be initialized.  For example, consider the following typedef ?statements:  typedef struct {  ? ? uint8_T bad1;  ? ? boolean_T bad2;  } SubBusType;    typedef struct {  ? ? SubBusType SubBus;  ? ? real_T f1;  ? ? real_T f2;  ? ? real_T f3;  ? ? // other fields from f4 to f320  } MainBusType;?  Suppose you initialize a variable FooSignal ?with these values:  static const MainBusType tmp = {  ? ? {?  ? ? ? ?1U,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* bad1 */  ? ? ? ?false? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* bad2 */  ? ? },? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? /* SubBus */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f1 */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f2 */  ? ? 10.0,? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?/* f3 */?  ? ? // other fields from f4 to f320  };  FooSignal = tmp;  The generated model initialization function might include code similar to the following: (void)memset(&FooSignal, 1, sizeof(MainBusType));  FooSignal.f1 = 10.0;  FooSignal.f2 = 10.0;?  FooSignal.f3 = 10.0;  // other field assignments from f4 to f320  This code does not correctly initialize SubBus.bad2 .

Summary: Using mod function with a fixed-point type input argument of 7 or 15 bits causes error during HDL code generation
Description: An error as shown below can occur during HDL code generation when the? mod ?function is called in the following way: in(:) = mod(in, fi(16384, 0, 15, 0))  where?in  is an unsigned fixed-point type with either a 7  or 15? bit word length and 0  fraction length. To fix the error, use a different word-length for storing in .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A for loop in generated code with narrow floating point parameters might execute a different number of times than the corresponding MATLAB for loop
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for MATLAB code that contains a for  loop that has parameters with the single ?or  half_t ?type. The generated code might execute the corresponding? for  loop a different number of times from the MATLAB code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Cannot use sim3d.actor angle property to transform actor texture
Description: You cannot use the Angle  property to transform an actor texture. For example, using this code does not rotate the texture specified in?texture.png ?the specified 45 deg (pi/4 rads). >> actor1 = sim3d.Actor();>> actor1.Texture = fullfile(pwd,'texture.png');?>> actor1.TextureTransform.Angle = pi/4;?
Workaround: Use an editor to rotate the texture image. Save the updated texture file. Then, use the sim3d.actor Texture property to specify the updated texture file.

Summary: Imprecise dectection of actor collisions in simulation 3D environment
Description: The Unreal Engine uses invisible collision meshes to detect collisions between actors. This mesh is not the same as an actor's physical mesh.? By default, the simulation 3D environment creates a simple collision mesh for every actor. The default simple collision mesh is generally larger than the physical mesh. Therefore, the simulation 3D environment can imprecisely detect actor collisions. For example, cones created on a large plane appear to float above the plane itself.??
Workaround: To more precisely detect actor collisions with a plane surface, consider using the createShape function to create a thin box. Use the thin box to represent the plane surface. Unreal Engine uses the box dimensions to create the collision mesh. For example, the following code snippet creates a thin horizontal box which can represent a plane surface.>> box = sim3d.Actor('ActorName','Box');>> box.createShape('box',[10 10 0.001]);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root outports that are directly driven by a bus signal with ExportedGlobal storage class might generate incorrect initialization
Description: The code generator might generate an incorrect initialization when?all ?of the following are true: The model contains a root outport that is driven directly by a bus signal. That bus signal has?ExportedGlobal ?storage class.  The code generator might assign an uninitialized value to the root outport in the model initialization function. For example, consider this generated code:? / * Model initialize function */  void simple_initialize(void)  {  / * Registration code */    / * block I/ O */    / * exported global signals */  {  myBus temp = {   0. 0F    };     myOut = temp;   }    {  real32_T rtb_speed;     / * SystemInitialize for BusCreator: '<S1>/ Auto Bus Creator v2' */   myOut. speed = rtb_speed;   }  }  ?The generated code initializes the bus signal?myOut.speed ?with the local uninitialized variable?rtb_speed .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Error when scheduling remote verification using a third-party job scheduler
Description: If you offload your Polyspace code verification to a cluster configured with a Parallel Computing Toolbox cluster profile, and you use a third-party job scheduler to schedule the remote verifications, the process throws an error message similar to this: ?Error: Cannot determine MJS Security Level for scheduler 'profileName'  Where profileName  is the cluster profile name.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic blocks.
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of the Saturation blocks?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0628) ?gives abnormal exit warning for Saturation Dynamic  blocks when upper or lower saturation limit inputs are not connected.

Summary: Unable to save top models in the Model Maintainability and Model Testing Dashboards
Description: If there are models with unsaved changes and you try to collect metric results in the?Model Maintainability ?or?Model Testing Dashboards , the Unsaved Changes dialog appears and you can click?Save and Continue ?to try to save the models. If a top model and at least one referenced model have unsaved changes, the dashboard is unable to save the top model and generates the error: The following files were not able to be saved: <Path to top model>
Workaround: Manually save the referenced models and the top model, or execute:save_system(<Name of top model>, SaveDirtyReferencedModels="on")

Summary: Executing the mcc command by specifying the -s and -j options together results in the auto-generated p-code not being packaged
Description: Executing the mcc? command with the -j  option generates p-code from the user's .m file and packages it into a deployable archive (.ctf  file).? Executing the mcc  command with the -s  option obfuscates folder structures and file names in the deployable archive (.ctf  file) from the end user. However, when executing the mcc  command by specifying the -s  and -j  options together, the resulting auto-generated p-code is not packaged. Instead, the .m files are packaged.

Summary: Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that new version of the source document is available after opening requirement set from Git repository
Description: When you use MATLAB to clone a Git repository and then open an imported requirement set in the Requirements Editor, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document in available. Similarly, when you pull the latest changes for an imported requirement set from Git and open it, the Requirements Editor incorrectly indicates that a new version of the source document is available.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b

Summary: Compatibility check for Test Generation might cause MATLAB to crash if model coverage objectives is 'Enhanced MCDC'
Description: The compatibility check for Test Generation causes MATLAB to crash if the following two conditions are true: Model coverage objectives is 'Enhanced MCDC' Model uses Simulink.AliasType object as type aliases for signal and parameter data types  In such situations, when building model representation for 'Enhanced MCDC' objectives, processing of a model element that uses input or output signal with type defined by Simulink.AliasType object causes MATLAB to crash.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: Saving model after analyzing model coverage for multiple different subsystem harnesses throws an error
Description: If you analyze coverage for multiple different subsystem harnesses, and then save the top model, you get an error message that looks like: Bad object handle (955) at index 1. Error in parameter number 2. Error using save_systemAn error occurred during a callback while saving <modelName>.  The previously saved version of this file (if any) has not been changed.   Caused by:    Error using save_system>i_save    **** Error **** - Show complete stack trace

Summary: Cannot edit variables using context menu in Simulink Model Explorer
Description: When viewing the contents of a variable in the Simulink Model Explorer, you cannot use the Duplicate, Insert, Delete, Rename, or Edit Value items in the context menu to edit the variable. You must make these edits programmatically or using the MATLAB Variables Editor.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results when struct containing matrix field, imported from custom code has field element of type long long
Description: Using struct containing matrix fields imported from custom code, having "long long" field types, can produce wrong simulation results when C Caller and C Function blocks are involved.

Summary: Type redefinition compiler error when simulating a model containing struct with ND array element
Description: If your custom code struct type has a tag?that is the struct name followed by a suffix "_tag", for example: typedef struct mystruct_tag{  int flag;  double data[4];  } mystruct;  Using such struct inside Simulink with blocks like C Caller and C Function can cause type re-definition error similar to:? 'mystruct_tag': 'struct' type redefinition

Summary: keyHash?function produces a collision if the only differences between numeric arrays are the locations of zeros
Description: Collisions occur if the only differences between numeric arrays are the locations of zeros. For example, these two calls to the keyHash  function return the same hash code. >> keyHash([0 1 2 3])>> keyHash([1 2 3 0])

Summary: The getAttribute function returns incorrect values for the PaintColorattribute
Description: If you use thegetAttribute(actorModel, 'PaintColor')  function of a Simulink.ActorModel object, the returned PaintColor  structure holds incorrect values. Specifically, the values of the?g , b  and a ?structure fields (green color, blue color, and color transparency, respectively)?are incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses deep learning libraries might produce incorrect answers if 1x1?convolution2dLayer?post-dominates?maxPooling2dLayer
Description: Generated code that uses MKL-DNN, ARM Compute or cuDNN library might produce incorrect answers if your deep learning network satisfies all  these conditions: The network contains a  maxPooling2dLayer ?followed by a  convolution2dLayer .  All paths in the layer graph that start at the  maxPooling2dLayer ?go through the  convolution2dLayer ?(that is, the? convolution2dLayer ?post-dominates ?the?maxPooling2dLayer ). The  convolution2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  equal to  [1 1], ?and ?has equal strides that are greater than  1 , and ?has no spatial padding .  The  maxPooling2dLayer ?has  FilterSize  greater than  1 , or ?has strides greater than  1 , or  has spatial padding.  The only layers between the? maxPooling2dLayer ?and  convolution2dLayer ?are  reluLayer, additionLayer, clippedReluLayer, leakyReluLayer.   This is an example network layer graph that might produce incorrect answers in code generation:? ?lg = layerGraph([imageInputLayer([5 5 3], Normalization = 'none', Name = 'input')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? maxPooling2dLayer([2 3], Name = 'maxpool')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? reluLayer(Name = 'relu')?  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? convolution2dLayer(1,3,Stride = [2 2], Name = 'conv')]);?  This issue affects code generated by using any  of these products: MATLAB Coder  Simulink Coder  GPU Coder when using  TargetLibrary='cudnn'?
Workaround: For code generation targeting Intel, ARM, or AMD based CPUs, use?TargetLibrary='none'.For code generation targeting NVIDIA GPUs, use TargetLibrary='tensorrt'.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Compilation error "undeclared identifier" when using inf/nan as mask parameter in a reusable subsystem during code generation
Description: Code generation produces a compilation error about an undeclared identifier rtInf  or rtNan  when you use inf/nan as a mask parameter in a reusable subsystem with these settings: Model configuration parameter DefaultParameterBehavior ?is set to Inlined .  The reusable subsystem with mask parameter is inside another reusable subsystem. For the reusable subsystem with the mask parameter, the subsystem block parameter RTWFileNameOpts  is not set to Auto.
Workaround: Set model configuration parameter CustomHeaderCode to #include rt_nonfinite.h

Summary: Large constants result in incorrect code generation for MATLAB Function block
Description: When constants that take a large amount of space to store (above 2 million bits) are used in a MATLAB Function block, HDL Coder prints:  /* unsupported construct */  in the generated VHDL instead of printing the constant.
Workaround: Add the constant block outside of the MATLAB Function block, and add the constant as an input to the function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for network containing depth-wise convolution with dilation values might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for a deep learning network that contains the depth-wise convolution with non-unity dilation factor might produce incorrect results. This issue affects the ARM Compute target.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: insEKF?incorrectly computes numeric Jacobian for custom sensor or custom motion models
Description: If you use an? insEKF  object with a custom sensor model or custom motion model, and that custom model implements a  stateTransition  method, but not?a  stateTransitionJacobian  method, the Jacobian is computed incorrectly.  These standard sensors and motion models are not affected by this issue: insMotionPose  insMotionOrientation  insGPS  insAccelerometer  insMagnetometer  insGyroscope .?
Workaround: For each custom sensor model or custom motion model used with an?insEKF, check that if the model?implements a?stateTransition?function, it also implements a stateTransitionJacobian function.

Summary: hdl.iteratorfun?might generate incorrect code when the input arguments contain non-streamed signal or streamed signal is passed as extra argument
Description: ?In the following situations, hdl.iteratorfun  may generate incorrect code. The generated model fails to run in these cases: 1. When using a streamed value,?aStreamedValue,?as a trailing argument to hdl.iteratorfun  as shown below. hdl.iteratorfun(@kernel, val, out, aStreamedValue)  Workaround: Use streamed inputs only as the second argument for hdl.iteratorfun. 2. When iterating over a non-streamed matrix type. hdl.iteratorfun(@kernel, nonStreamedValue, out)  nonStreamedValue could be a value computed by another hdl.iteratorfun or a non-streamed input signal to the design.? Workaround: Use only a vector for nonStreamedValue .? 3. When the valid signal is toggled, if you are iterating over a non-streamed input. hdl.iteratorfun(@kernel, nonStreamedValue, out)?

Summary: RoadRunner Scenario Reader and Writer blocks output incorrect pose values when Topic is set to Actor Pose (Driving Scenario Compatible)
Description: When the?Topic ?parameter of a RoadRunner?Scenario Reader or Writer block is set to?Actor Pose?(Driving Scenario Compatible) , the actor pose is converted between a 4-by-4?Pose  matrix  and Roll, Pitch, Yaw, and Position ?values. The conversion uses an incorrect sequence of rotations which causes Roll  and Pitch  angles to get swapped.

Summary: "Index exceeds the number of array elements" warning thrown when authoring AUTOSAR architecture models while XML Options dialog is open
Description: If the XML Options dialog is open while adding ports to an AUTOSAR architecture model, a warning similar to the following might be thrown to the command line: Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 0.?

Summary: Error when copying a block in a nested subsystem reference might crash Simulink
Description: If an error occurs when copying a block inside a nested subsystem reference, Simulink might crash.?
Workaround: Possible fixes are:Install the product required for the blockUpdate the copy callback of the block so it does not produce an error

Summary: Simulink Check validation test cases SLCHK_HI_Q026, SLCHK_HI_Q101, and SLCHK_MS_Q002 fail when optional licenses are not installed
Description: When Simulink Requirements is not installed, these test cases?fail with error mathworks.hism.hisl_0070 is not a valid check ID : SLCHK_HI_Q026 : m_hism_hisl_0070_PASS ? SLCHK_HI_Q026 : m_hism_hisl_0070_WARN   When Simulink Design Verifier is not installed, these test cases fail with error mathworks.hism.0101 is not a valid check ID : SLCHK_HI_Q101 : m_hism_hisl_0101_PASS   SLCHK_HI_Q101 : m_hism_hisl_0101_WARN?   When Stateflow is not installed, test case SLCHK_MS_Q002 : MdlMetricsInfo_PASS  fails with error mathworks.iec61508.MdlMetricsInfo is not a valid check ID .?
Workaround: Open a new MATLAB session and go to?a location where you have write access. On the MATLAB command line, enter hKitProjectSetup('iec', 'slchk');Open?certkitiec_slchk_tests.xlsx.Locate test cases?SLCHK_HI_Q026,?SLCHK_HI_Q101, and?SLCHK_MS_Q002?(column C) and delete the row for each test case.Save the spreadsheet.Open certkitiec_slchk_run.mlx and follow the workflow to execute the test procedure.

Summary: Error when generating report from a System Composer model containing Adapter block
Description: Generating a report from a System Composer model that contains an Adapter block may produce the following error: Error in line: append(rpt,  ComponentElements);?Unrecognized function or variable 'id'.

Summary: Admin Center fails to start on Linux
Description: When you start Admin Center with the admincenter command on a Linux operating system, you might encounter the following error: . / admincenter: 165: . / admincenter: / bin/ java: not found
Workaround: To work around this issue,?set the MJS_JAVA environment variable before you start Admin Center. Execute the following command:export MJS_JAVA=<matlabroot>/sys/java/jre/glnxa64/jre?<matlabroot> is the path to the root folder of your MATLAB installation.

Summary: MATLAB hangs if MQTT subscribe and write calls are made simultaneously
Description: If an MQTT client is subscribing to multiple topics and any client publishes into those same topics while the subscriptions are establishing, MATLAB hangs after a few subscription calls.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add a pause?of 0.2s?or more, after each subscription call.
Products: Instrument Control Toolbox, Industrial Communication Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Driving a variable-size root outport with a fixed-size signal from an Enabled Subsystem block produces incorrect answer in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Rapid accelerator mode might produce an incorrect answer when you connect a fixed-size signal from an Enabled Subsystem block to a variable-size root outport. Simulink might not initialize the root outport and produce an incorrect value for the signal.

Summary: Review scope values in Configuration Settings section of Polyspace report are incorrect
Description: If you generate a Polyspace report and all the following are true: You use option Only include currently displayed results .
