Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code that passes?varargout?as an input to?strcmp?might produce incorrect C/C++ code
Description: MATLAB code generation incorrectly handles the situation in which varargout  is passed as an input to strcmp .   For example, consider this MATLAB function:?  function [cmp, varargout] = examplevarargout = {'a','b'};cmp = strcmp('a', varargout);end In this situation, the generated code behaves as if the result of strcmp  is always false . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Before using strcmp, do either of these:Save varargout to a local variableUse curly braces to extract individual elements of varargout

Summary: Some UIs appear blank when accessing via Remote Desktop
Description: When using tools like Remote Desktop or TeamViewer to remotely access a Windows machine, some UIs can appear blank. Affected UIs include, but are not limited to: App Designer AddOns Explorer AddOns Manager Simulink Test Manager Simulink Data Inspector? Any UI produced with UIFigure or App Designer  The UIs run, and the window appears with the correct tile, but the contents of the window remain blank.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, run the following commands as the first commands in a MATLAB session:wm = matlab.internal.webwindowmanager.instance;wm.setStartupOptions('ExternalProcess', '--ignore-gpu-blacklist --disable-gpu-compositing');?You can add these commands to a?user-defined startup script?to ensure that they run early in the MATLAB startup process.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Unrecognized method, property, or field error during Model block SIL/PIL simulation
Description: Consider a Model block with a referenced model that logs a signal. If the logged signal implementation resolves to a custom storage class, then running a simulation with block parameter Simulation mode  set to ?Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?can produce this error:? Unrecognized method, property, or field 'BaseRegion' for class 'coder.descriptor.BasicAccessFunctionExpression'?  ?
Workaround: Identify the logged signal that is causing the issue.Configure the logged signal as a test point. For more information, see?Configure Signals as Test Points.

Summary: Unable to view custom UI component in App Designer Component Library if the description for the component contains newlines
Description: When configuring a custom UI component to be usable from App Designer, if the description field contains newlines, the configuration will successfully complete but the component will not appear in the App Designer Component Library.
Workaround: Reconfigure the custom UI component by launching the App Designer Custom UI Component Metadata dialog. Remove any newlines from the description field and click OK.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting a composite data interface or value type in software architecture models
Description: In software architecture models, deleting a composite data interface or value type associated with a function argument of a service interface might produce unexpected behavior, such as a MATLAB crash.
Workaround: Change the type of the function argument to a different type than the composite data interface or the value type that is going to be deleted.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might output values from the current time step instead of the previous time step
Description: The Roadrunner Scenario Reader block should output actor or vehicle poses from the previous time step. However, if a Simulink model has both Roadrunner Scenario Reader and RoadRunner Scenario Writer blocks, the Roadrunner Scenario Reader block might incorrectly output pose values written in the current time step by the?RoadRunner Scenario Writer block.? This situation might occur if the Roadrunner Scenario Writer block, through its message lines or signal input lines, cannot be traced back to the RoadRunner Scenario Reader block.

Summary: spectrumAnalyzer release method if called after hide does not unlock the object
Description: If the spectrumAnalyzer object is provided with data, then hide method was called followed by release, the scope will remain locked preventing any changes to non-tunable properties.
Workaround: Before calling release method on spectrumAnalyzer object, if hide was called, call show method on the scope then call release.?

Summary: Report Explorer System Snapshot component creates blank image
Description: If you set the Report Explorer System Snapshot component options to generate a Windows metafile (EMF) image in landscape orientation, the component produces a blank image.? The component uses landscape orientation if?any of these conditions are true: The Orientation ? option is set to Landscape  .  The Orientation ?option is set to?Use system orientation ?and the system's PaperOrientation ?parameter is set to landscape . The Orientation  option is set to?Largest dimension vertical  and the system's layout is wider than it is tall.
Workaround: Use an image format other than Windows metafile:For Word reports intended for Word 2016 or later versions, use the SVG format for the highest quality image.?Earlier versions of Word do not support SVG images, use PNG format instead.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assertion can occur during HDL code generation when using a Gain block and enabling adaptive pipelining
Description: An internal assertion can occur when you generate HDL code for a model that has: A Gain block? Adaptive Pipelining  set to on ? The Synthesis Tool  and Target Frequency  set?  The assertion that occurs is the following:matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\compcoreop.cpp:406:Input signal to Gain block is empty
Workaround: To avoid this assertion, either:Model the Gain block as a Product block by moving the gain value to a Constant block and setting it as one of the inputs for the Product block.Disable adaptive pipelining.

Summary: Computing frequency-domain features in the Diagnostic Feature Designer app using imported spectrum data generates an error
Description: When you import your own spectrum data into theDFD app using the New Session dialog box, the app does not assign the samplingfrequency correctly. This problem is not apparent until you try to use thatspectrum data to compute a feature option from the Frequency-Domain Featuresmenu. The conflict between the correct sampling frequency and the incorrect appversion of the sampling frequency causes the app to throw errors in the commandwindow.? The error message includes the following: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ModeChanged defined for class ctrluis.toolstrip.ModeManager:Error using predmaintguis.internal.ciapp.plots.frequencydomainfeatures.SpectralFeaturesMode/computeDependentSpectrumStatesError setting property 'MaxFrequency' of class 'predmaint.internal.options.SpectralFeatureOptions'. Value must be positive.

Summary: Wrong initial values in simulation results for export-function model with bus element port and Mux block at the root level
Description: Incorrect zero values are reported in simulation results for the first time step in normal mode simulation when all ?of the following conditions apply: The model is an export-function model. The model contains an In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model contains a Mux block at the root level. The Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to Array , Structure , or Structure with time .  The zero values are reported instead of the initial values of the signals that drive the root Out Bus Element and Outport blocks.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Generating CUDA code for?pcfitcuboid produces a compile-time error for GPU compute capability greater than or equal to 6.1 and single type
Description: Generating CUDA code for the pcfitcuboid ?function throws the following compile-time error when the inputs are of the single data type and the GPU compute capability is greater than or equal to 6.1 . Error:?  Inputs to gpucoder. atomicAdd function must be of the same data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior when periodic function-call subsystem uses invalid periodic sample time
Description: Updating a model produces an incorrect error when all of these conditions are true: A periodic function-call subsystem has a Trigger block with Sample time type  set to periodic . In this periodic function-call subsystem, a block explicitly specifies a different periodic sample time. This periodic function-call subsystem is one of multiple subsystems in the block diagram.  The incorrect error is similar to this: The enabled subsystem 'subsystem-name' executes in multiple tasks.  This can cause corrupted data or non-deterministic behavior in a real-time system.  Building a model that produces this incorrect error might generate incorrect code.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Code generation might throw error when invoking uminus on logical dlarray
Description: Code generation might produce an error if your MATLAB code invokes?uminus  on a logical dlarray . For example, when generating code for: out = -dlarray(true);?  the code generator produces this error: This assignment writes a 'double' value into a 'logical' type. Code generation does not support changing types through assignment. Check preceding assignments or input?type specifications for type mismatches.
Workaround: Convert the logical?dlarray to double before invoking uminus. For example,out = -double(dlarray(true));?

Summary: HDL IP core generates code with AXI3 interface for Microchip SmartFusion2 board?in HDL Workflow Advisor.
Description: Using IP core generation workflow in HDL Workflow Advisor for Microchip SmartFusion2 board, the SmartFusion2 based IP core are created with AXI3 interface instead of AXI4 interface. HDL Workflow Advisor infrastructure only supports the AXI4 based Interface.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for export-function model with State Writer block that writes to Outport block
Description: Simulating an export-function model produces incorrect results when all these conditions apply: The?Data Import/Export > Format ?configuration parameter is set to?Dataset . The model contains two root Inport blocks that output function-call signals. Each function-call signal connects to a function-call port of a function-call subsystem. The first function-call subsystem contains a State Writer block. The State Writer block writes to an Outport block in the second function-call subsystem. The corresponding output port directly drives a root Outport block.  The output of the root Outport block is logged each time either function-call subsystem executes. When the first function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged incorrectly. When the second function-call subsystem executes, the output of the root Outport block is logged correctly.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Build error due to incorrect preprocessor if conditions guarding function declaration and definition
Description: A build error can occur when all the following conditions are met :?A modeling element generates functions - e.g Stateflow charts This modeling element is contained within an inlined variant region. For example, inside a choice of a variant subsystem, with the FunctionPackaging  set to Inline . This variant region is triggered by a function call output from a reusable Stateflow chart.  Example error message: myModel1.c: In function 'myModel1_Pos':  myModel1.c:103:18: error: 'D_Work_myModel1' has no member named 'is_c2_myModel1'  myModel1_DWork.is_c2_myModel1 = myModel1_IN_Neg;
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may return incorrect results for a deep learning network with a LeakyReluLayer with 'Scale' property set to negative
Description: Code generated for a SeriesNetwork , DAGNetwork , or dlnetwork  with a?LeakyReluLayer ?may produce incorrect results if both? conditions are true: The 'Scale ' property of?LeakyReluLayer ?is set to negative. Target library is set to? 'none '.
Workaround: There are a few walkaround options to resolve this issue:1. Generate code for a different target such as mkldnn.2. If you want to use target none, you can replace the?LeakyReluLayer?in the network with a custom layer that performs a leaky reLU operation.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating, updating, or generating code for a model with a Function Element block without elements
Description: In Simulink, if you create a Function Element block and delete the function element name from the block label, MATLAB might crash when the model is simulated, updated, or used to generate code.

Summary: SimBiology projects lose data and become corrupted when apps are left open overnight
Description: If you leave a SimBiology project open overnight in the SimBiology Model Builder  app or the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, the project might lose data and become corrupted. This data loss can lead to errors when saving or loading the project or when trying to load data or results from the project. This corruption happens because the operating system sometimes deletes temporary files needed by the SimBiology project.
Workaround: You can prevent the operating system from deleting SimBiology temporary files by changing the MATLAB temporary folder. Set the appropriate environment variable to a different location, such as your documents folder. On Windows, set the environment variable TMP. On macOS or Linux, set the environment variable TMPDIR.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB may crash when destroying multiple copies of a handle object whose destructor accesses the caller's workspace
Description: If the user creates an instance of a handle class whose destructor accesses the caller's workspace, then creates a copy of that instance, MATLAB may crash when destroying those variables. MATLAB may not crash every time those variables are destroyed. These crashes may contain the following lines at the top of the stack trace (the stack shown here is from a Windows machine): [ 0] 0x00007ffaf364c867 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+08308839 FeatureTestObservableWorkspace+00211111  [ 1] 0x00007ffaf35ae934 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+07661876 MathWorks::lxe::profiler::MexFunctionCall::EnterPoint+00180036  [ 2] 0x00007ffaf363fce9 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+08256745 FeatureTestObservableWorkspace+00159017  [ 3] 0x00007ffaf363d22b bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+08245803 FeatureTestObservableWorkspace+00148075  [ 4] 0x00007ffaf363cea5 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+08244901 FeatureTestObservableWorkspace+00147173  [ 5] 0x00007ffaf35dca82 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+07850626 MathWorks::lxe::profiler::MexFunctionCall::EnterPoint+00368786  [ 6] 0x00007ffaf3628d16 bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+08162582 FeatureTestObservableWorkspace+00064854  To demonstrate the bug, download the two files attached to this Bug Report and run the reproduceCrash function. It may take multiple runs of reproduceCrash to demonstrate the bug.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when connecting client or server ports to variant components
Description: Client and server ports are not supported for variant components in a software architecture model. Connecting a client or server port to a variant component might produce unexpected behavior such as a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Unused variable compiler warning if a temporary variable is used in only one branch of an if condition
Description: Compiler warnings about unused variables might occur for a model that meets all  of these conditions:The model contains a signal with the GetSet  Storage Class. At least one logical operator takes the signal as an input.  For a model that satisfies the above conditions, the code generator might produce code like following:? void  Repro_without_variant_Fcn (void) { ? ModeEnum tmp;  ? tmp = In1;  ? if (In1 == Mode_1) {  ? ? ...  ? } else {  ? ? ...  ? ? if (get_In2() && (tmp != Mode_2))  ? ? {  ? ? ? rtb_Switch2 = Repro_without_variant_U.In4;  ? ? } else {  ? ? ? rtb_Switch2 = 0;  ? ? }  ? }  }   The generated code initializes the temporary variable?tmp? but uses it in? only one branch of the if  condition? This might cause a C or C++ compiler warning?similar to the following: warning: variable tmp was set but never used .?

Summary: Incorrect external mode simulation results when C Caller block drives Parameter Writer block in an initialize function
Description: Simulation results are wrong for a model when all the following conditions are true: A C Caller or C Function block drives a Parameter Writer block. The Parameter Writer block is?in an Initialize Function block. The model runs in external mode, for example, the model runs on a ZedBoard.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a

Summary: mdfWrite function might write to the wrong file with the same name on MATLAB path
Description: When attempting to write to a non-existent MDF-file while another file with the same name exists on the MATLAB path, the mdfWrite  function might mistakenly write to the existing file rather than create and write to the intended file.

Summary: polyspace-configure errors when reading build trace on Windows
Description: If you run polyspace-configure ?on Windows, the command might fail sporadically when attempting to read internal files while Polyspace reads the build trace. The command outputs a message similar to to this: polyspace-configure: 24s: ERROR: Error reading build trace line 1: Permission deniedpolyspace-configure: 24s: ERROR: With Windows error: 33   The issue can happen during the compilation phase or, for some compilers, when Polyspace looks for the compiler include folders on your system.
Workaround: You can work around this issue in most cases by clearing the compiler cache and then rerunning the polyspace-configure command:? ? ? polyspace-configure -clear-compiler-cache? ? ? polyspace-configure <ps_options> <build_cmd>If the issue happens frequently while Polyspace is attempting to detect the compiler include folders:Specify the compiler include folders manually in your project.Use option -include-strategy none to disable the detection of compiler include folders:polyspace-configure -include-strategy none <other ps_options> <build_cmd>?

Summary: Synthesis attribute for DSP style is not generated when synthesis tool is set to Quartus Pro
Description: When you specify the?DSPStyle ? to on ?for Product blocks in a model and set the?SynthesisTool  to Intel Quartus Pro , no synthesis attribute is generated in the HDL code.
Workaround: Synthesis attributes are generated in the HDL code if the synthesis tool is set to Altera Quartus II for the code generation session.>> makehdl(<model/dut>, 'SynthesisTool', 'Altera Quartus II')?

Summary: Satellite scenario viewer visualization for receiver object's antenna radiation pattern is not properly updated when specifying a new frequency value
Description: Consider a receiver object and visualize the radiation pattern of this receiver in a satellite scenario viewer using the pattern function. % Set up the scenariosc = satelliteScenario;??sat = satellite(sc, 1e7, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0);?rx = receiver(sat);?viewer = satelliteScenarioViewer(sc);  % Visualize the radiation pattern  pattern(rx, 3e9); % This will plot a radiation pattern at the specified frequency in an open satellite scenario viewer?  Now, visualize the radiation pattern of the same receiver object using a different frequency value. You will see that the radiation pattern's visuals in the satellite scenario viewer will not update and will instead remain the same. pattern(rx, 3e8); % This is supposed to update the radiation pattern visualization, but due to this bug the visual will not be updated
Workaround: The pattern object can be deleted before the second call to the pattern function to allow the visualization to properly update, like :pat1 = pattern(rx, 3e9);?delete(pat1); % This will remove the visualization?pat2 = pattern(rx, 3e8); % The new visualization will properly reflect the new frequency of the radiation pattern

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check updates to variables used in state transition conditions?throws?abnormal exit warning for function calls in Stateflow charts
Description: Model Advisor check Check updates to variables used in state transition conditions?(ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0741 )? throws abnormal exit warnings for function calls that are not assigned to an output variable?in Stateflow charts.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multi-Input Sum Block that is configured with mixed positive and negative sign combinations may result in a numerical mismatch
Description: HDL Coder generates incorrect HDL code, when your model has Multi-Input Sum Block?that is configured with mixed positive (+) & negative (-) sign combinations, such as (+, -, +), (+, +, -) and more. The generated HDL code may result in potential numerical mismatch.?
Workaround: Consider modeling Multi-Input Sum Block?in terms of series of Two Input Adder Blocks.

Summary: GPU Coder?incorrectly checks for Image Processing Toolbox license when using the?imresize?function
Description: imresize ?is a MATLAB function that does not require Image Processing Toolbox.? When generating CUDA code for the?imresize ?function, GPU Coder incorrectly checks for ?a valid? Image Processing Toolbox?license.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Numerical mismatch between normal mode and SIL/PIL simulations when models use connected Inport and Lookup Table blocks
Description: ?Consider a model that has Inport blocks that connect to Lookup Table blocks. In addition: The model uses right-click build, export-function subsystem blocks or export-function blocks with asynchronous inputs. The input signals to the Lookup Table blocks are arrays.  During a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation, input port values might not be transferred from the development computer to the target application, which can result in a numerical mismatch with normal mode results.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block between each Inport block and Lookup Table block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator generates additional transposes when preserving element dimensions for bus elements in row-major models with S-Functions
Description: The code generator incorrectly generates additional transposes when?all ?of the following are true: The Array layout  model configuration parameter is set to Row-major . The model contains an S-Function block that has at least one inport or outport of Simulink.Bus  data type. The Simulink.Bus  data type used by an S-Function block has its Preserve element dimensions  property set to true.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: An unused struct field or class property assigned with a function call that has side effects might generate incorrect code
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all? these conditions:  You assigned the result of a function call to a class property or a struct field. This function has side effects. (A function has side effects if the function modifies something and that change is visible outside the scope of the function.) The class property or struct field is unused in your code.  An example MATLAB function affected by this issue: function out = fcn(u)    s = struct('a',[u u*4 u],'b',u+2,'c',u,'d',u*2);    s1 = [s s];    s.d = fcn2(s1); % function call with side effect    out = s1(2).b + s1(1).c;end
Workaround: Perform either of these actions:?If you have Embedded Coder license, enable the configuration setting Preserve unused fields and properties?(PreserveUnusedStructFields).cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.PreserveUnusedStructFields = true;In your MATLAB code, before the assignment of the unused struct field or class property, assign the result of the function call to a temporary variable. Then use this temporary variable to assign the field or property. For example, modify the example code in the previous section as follows:function out = fcn(u)    s = struct('a',[u u*4 u],'b',u+2,'c',u,'d',u*2);    s1 = [s s];    tmp = fcn2(s1); % assign function call with side effect to temporary variable    s.d = tmp;    out = s1(2).b + s1(1).c;end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating nonreusable C++ code from an export function model does not wrap Simulink Functions in extern C functions
Description: If you generate nonreusable C++ code from an export function model, the code generator does not wrap the external functions in extern  C functions. The code generator still wraps global data and exported functions in extern  C functions.

Summary: Image generation experiment template errors
Description: The Experiment Manager template "Image Generation Using Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)" calls the function projectAndReshapeLayer ?using an incorrect syntax that results in an error.
Workaround: In Experiment Manager, edit line 28 of the training function, to call projectAndReshapeLayer using this syntax:projectAndReshapeLayer(projectionSize,'Name','proj');

Summary: The return of ModelAdvisor.run does not account for the number of justified checks
Description: ModlelAdvisor.run ? returns ModelAdvisor.SystemResult  as anoutput argument. However,?ModelAdvisor.SystemResult? doesnot account for the number of justified checks.

Summary: Booting of the Microchip Linux Image on PolarFire SoC Icicle Kit is not working properly.
Description: Microchip Linux image does not boot properly on the PolarFire SoC Icicle Kit because Microcontroller Sub-System (MSS) is not configured with UART terminals, eMMC and SD card settings.

Summary: Direct assignment to structures greater than 2^32 bytes leads to internal error
Description: Assignment to data structures greater than or equal to 2^32 bytes leads to an internal error. For example, in the following code snippet, struct X  has a size greater than this limit. Direct assignment between two variables of type?struct X ?leads to the error. struct X {   int d[268435456];};struct X x0;struct X x = x0; // causes crash

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports compatibilityerror while adding a coverage filter
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports the error: The top model <top_model> and the referenced model<ref_model> or one of the models it directly or indirectlyreferences have different Format parameter settings. To eliminate this error,update top model <top_model> and all models referenced directlyor indirectly by this top model to use Dataset format.   This behavior is seen when followingconditions are met: Model uses model references andall models in the hierarchy use Dataset   as the value for SaveFormat  configuration parameter A coverage filter is added using CovFilter  configuration parameter while generating tests using SimulinkDesign Verifier

Summary: insEKF/fuse?object function might output state with unnormalized quaternion
Description: The?fuse ?function of the?insEKF ? object returns two outputs: state and state covariance.?? If you use the INS filter to track an orientation quaternion, the quaternion in the state output of the fuse ?object? function might be unnormalized or have a negative real part.?
Workaround: Use the State and StateCovariance properties saved in the insEKF object instead of the outputs of the fuse object function because the quaternion saved in the State property of the insEKF object is normalized and has a positive real part.

Summary: Crash when specifying a value for a Simulink.Parameter object by using a?SimulationInput object
Description: When you use a Simulink.SimulationInput  object to provide a new value for a variable of type Simulink.Parameter  which has a custom data type that you specify in a data dictionary, running the simulation using the SimulationInput  object might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Deleted instrumented signals inside subsystem reference blocks reappear when subsystem or model reloads
Description: Even after deleting and saving, instrumented signals inside a subsystem reference block reappear when you reload the subsystem or model. This situation might occur as a result of one or more of these conditions: Multiple models refer to the same subsystem file in which instrumented signals are present.? Nested subsystem references and instrumented signals are in the inner subsystem reference. You open the subsystem file when no other file referring to it is open, delete the instrumented signals, and save the subsystem. You open one of the models referring to the instrumented subsystem file, delete the instrumented signals, and save the subsystem.
Workaround: After making changes to instrumented signals, save all the files referring to the subsystem files separately.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: for loops in MATLAB code are?incorrectly parallelized into GPU kernels
Description: Incorrect code might generate for MATLAB code that meets all ?of the following conditions: A function of the MATLAB code contains for  loops That function contains coder.gpu.kernelfun  pragma to parallelize the loops into GPU kernels if they pass the parallel-loop analysis check  For example, consider this MATLAB Code: function y = design  ? ? c = MyClass(zeros(1000,1));  ? ? S.a = c;  ? ? S.b = c;  ? ? y = foo(S);  end  function y = foo(S)  ? ? coder.inline('never');  ? ? coder.gpu.kernelfun;  ? ? % Loop must not be parallel if S.a and S.b refer to the same memory location  ? ? for i = 1:10  ? ? ? ? S.b.x(i+1) = S.a.x(i) + 1;  ? ? end  ? ? y = S.a.x(10);  end  Calling the MATLAB function design  produces an output of 9 .  The generated code for function?design ?is incorrectly? parallelized as follows: static real_T foo(const MyClass *S_a, MyClass *S_b)  {? MyClass *gpu_S_a;? MyClass *gpu_S_b;? real_T y;? cudaMalloc(&gpu_S_b, 8000UL);? cudaMalloc(&gpu_S_a, 8000UL);? //??Loop must not be parallel if S.a and S.b refer to the same memory? //? location? cudaMemcpy(gpu_S_a, S_a, 8000UL, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);? cudaMemcpy(gpu_S_b, S_b, 8000UL, cudaMemcpyHostToDevice);? foo_kernel1<<<dim3(1U, 1U, 1U), dim3(32U, 1U, 1U)>>>(gpu_S_a, gpu_S_b);? y = S_a->x[9];? cudaMemcpy(S_b, gpu_S_b, 8000UL, cudaMemcpyDeviceToHost);? cudaFree(gpu_S_a);? cudaFree(gpu_S_b);? return y;}?  Calling the generated code?produces an incorrect output of?0 .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Connecting a root inport to an Initialize Function block might produce incorrect code
Description: The code generator will produce incorrect code when you connect a root inport to an Initialize Function block and the data type of the root inport is any ?of the following: An enumerated type whose default enumeration has a nonzero integer value A fixed-point type with nonzero bias A bus type that has an element whose data type is one of the preceding categories  The generated code will incorrectly overwrite the inport value before referencing it. For example: model_U.In1 = -5;  model_DW.A = modul_U.In1;
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Generated ARXML violates constr_1288 of the AUTOSAR standard
Description: When generating ARXML containing DataPrototypes of CATEGORY DATA_REFERENCE a SW-CALIBRATION-ACCESS is defined. This violates?constr_1288 of the AUTOSAR standard and can lead to issues when using the generated ARXML with third party tools.?
Workaround: Post process the generated ARXML to remove the offending SW-CALIBRATION-ACCESS nodes.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Indexing with colon expression returns incorrect result if colon overflow occurs
Description: In the generated code, an array indexing operation with a colon expression returns incorrect result if all  these conditions are true:  The colon expression has the form?start:step:start+v ?? The data type of? star t is one of?these:? int8 ,?int16 ,?uint8 ,?uint16  Either?start ? or?start+v ?overflows  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function out = colonOverflow(x)m = int8(x);a = 1:2000;out = a(m:1:m+2);end In MATLAB, the colon indexing operation saturates on overflow and returns 127. >> colonOverflow(127)  ans =  ? ?127  In generated code, the same function call returns an incorrect result. For example, if you generate a MEX function and run the generated MEX with the same input, you get:? >> colonOverflow_mex(127)  ans =  ? ?127? ?127? ?127  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening an X3D file with an unsupported node
Description: MATLAB crashes when you open an X3D file that contains an unsupported node placed in the 'Scene' node.?
Workaround: Remove unsupported nodes from the X3D file.? For more information, see?X3D Support?in the documentation.

Summary: Bug Finder fails on Windows with the message: Unable to find bfm file
Description: On Windows, Bug Finder reports the following error: **************************************************************** Beginning Bug-finder - Module Analysis***************************************************************** Bug-finder - Module Analysis - 1 (Computing modules)Error: The current working directory does not exist.Error: Unable to find bfm file: C:\TEMP\bfm\a\b\c\res\ALL\polyspace.bfm. This error occurs when you do not have access permission to the Polyspace results folder or one of its parent folders. Typically, the results folder is specified by using the? option?-results-dir .?
Workaround: You can use Microsoft provided tools such as?icacls.exe,?AccessChk, or?Process Monitor to inspect folder access rights and locate the folder to which you do not have access permission.Then run Polyspace with the option -results-dir specifying a folder for which you have access permission.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: SIL and PIL simulations might throw error message Found multiple matching Parameters
Description: Running a SIL or PIL simulation might throw the error message Found multiple matching Parameters ?when all ?of the following conditions are met:  You run a SIL or PIL simulation on a component that contains Prelookup blocks. The Prelookup blocks share Simulink.Breakpoint ?data between multiple lookup tables.

Summary: Generated code for interp1 with interpolation method set to?'pchip' or 'makima'?might run slower than MATLAB
Description: Suppose that: You generate code for the function interp1  with interpolation method set to 'pchip'  or 'makima'  During code generation, you disable the configuration parameter Check memory integrity ?(IntegrityChecks )   In such situations, the generated code might run slower when compared to MATLAB. The performance issue affects both MEX and standalone code generation.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code that uses length(varargout) or passes varargout to the other functions and then increases the size of varargout?might produce incorrect code
Description: The code generator might produce code that incorrectly computes the size of the cell array?varargout ?if all  these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code contains the call?length(varargout) ?or passes varargout ?to another function that you wrote, for example?foo(varargout) .  You then grow the variable?varargout ?to a bigger size by using subscripted assignments such as  varargout{3} = value .  Some execution paths of your MATLAB code do not include this growth operation. For example, this could happen if both  the previous operations are wrapped in a for  or while  loop.  In such situations, the new size of varargout ?after it grows might not be ?reflected in the generated code.? For example, consider this MATLAB code:? function [out, out2] = xGrowNargoutSWA(n)[out, out2, ~, ~] = local(n);endfunction [out, varargout] = local(n)out = 0;varargout{1} = 0;varargout{2} = 0;for i = 1:n    out = length(varargout);    varargout{3} = 0;endend The code generated for xGrowNargoutSWA  incorrectly returns the value of out  as 2 . The correct value of out  is either 2  or 3  depending on the value of n .??

Summary: Incorrect version of Simulink Coder in the generated A2L file
Description: The comment banner in A2L file generated using coder. asap2. export function contains Simulink version number in place of Simulink Coder version. From A2L files generated in R2022a using coder. asap2. export():  * Simulink Coder version : 10. 5 R2022a  * C source code generated on : Thu Jul 21 12:36:04 2022 Expected version of Simulink Coder is? "9.7?R2022a"

Summary: Error with C Function block and C Caller block when using MATLAB structure as parameter value where Simulink Bus type is expected
Description: When using a C Function block or a C Caller block, if a MATLAB structure is passed to a parameter that expects a Simulink bus, Simulink shows the following error message: Parameter data type mismatch in C Function block. Block parameter <parameter variable> of type <Bus: parameter bus> does not match type of evaluated parameter < parameter variable >.
Workaround: Use a Simulink.Parameter that represents the MATLAB structure parameter value.<parameter variable> = Simulink.Parameter(<parameter variable>)<parameter variable>.DataType = 'Bus: <parameter bus>'

Summary: Error when running parsim on a cluster that uses a third-party job scheduler
Description: When you run parsim  on a third-party scheduler cluster such as a Slurm cluster, Simulink is unable to find the project on the parallel workers. The simulation fails with the following error message:? Error loading project on parallel workers.  ? ? Error using parallel.pool/getPerHostFolder  ? ? The folder project was not found.
Workaround: Modify the communicatingJobWrapper.sh wrapper script found in the PluginScriptsLocation property of the cluster profile. Add the following line immediately before the call to mpiexec:unset HOST HOSTNAME
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: On Linux systems some windows display as blank when running MATLAB as root
Description: On Linux systems, some windows display as blank white windows when MATLAB is started as root or with sudo . The features work correctly, and the windows display their proper content when run as a regular user. The following can be affected: App Designer UIFigure Simulink Start Page Simulink Data Inspector AddOns Explorer
Workaround: Add the following command to your startup.m file or run it as the first command in a MATLAB session:setenv('MW_CEF_STARTUP_OPTIONS', '--in-process-gpu');

Summary: Reshapes from a 2-D matrix type to another 2-D matrix type generate incorrect HDL code
Description: A reshape from a 2-D matrix type to another generates indexing based on one of the matrix types for both the destination and source matrices. For example,?if x  is an 8-by-2 matrix and y  is:?? ?y =? reshape(x, [ 4 4 ]);? the  generated VHDL code is incorrect. This incorrect code results in an out-of-bounds indexing error when you attempt to compile the generated HDL code.??
Workaround: Rewrite the code so that one of the variables is stored in a 1-D matrix.

Summary: Compilation error in C++ generated code due to missing pointer qualifier in ert_main.cpp
Description: Generated code for a model containing a port-scoped Simulink function produces a compiler error under the following conditions: The port-scoped Simulink function has an input argument with a bus type The ERT system target file is selected The target language is set to C++  Under these conditions, the code generator generates a class in ert_main.cpp with a missing pointer qualifier in the prototype for the port-scoped Simulink function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Charts using MATLAB as the action language and configured to generate coverage report may see the duration operator return an incorrect result
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect for a Stateflow chart that: uses MATLAB as the action language is configured to generate a coverage report has a state that uses the?duration? operator to count elapsed time for some condition  the elapsed time returned by the?duration? operator for that condition may be incorrect after that condition first evaluated to true? continuously for a time period longer 255 simulation steps, and a transition was taken out of that state at the end of that time period. C code generated from these charts is not affected by this bug.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder generates loops that run with an incorrect number of iterations in specific cases
Description: When generating code using GPU Coder, loops authored in MATLAB might translate to loops or CUDA kernels that run with an incorrect number of iterations or threads. This can happen under the following conditions: The loop uses an index variable of integer  data?type . The loop uses a stride value other than 1 and the number of iterations is 0. In this case, the generated code might incorrectly run an iteration of the loop.(or) The total number of iterations of the loop exceeds the maximum value of the index variable type.  Example:? for i = int8(-127):n  ??? ...  end  Assume in the loop above that variable 'n'  has type 'int8' . Then, if n > 0 , the number of iterations of the loop will exceed intmax('int8') . Hence, the loop will run with an incorrect number of iterations in the generated code.
Workaround: To avoid generating code with incorrect number of iterations or threads, do?one of the following:Use the?double?data type for the?loop index variable.Rewrite the loop such that the start and step values of the loop are both 1.

Summary: Polyspace compilation error with elision of defaulted constructor
Description: If you use the "= default " specifier on a constructor that is then elided (omitted) through compiler optimizations, a Polyspace analysis of this code using versions C++11 or C++14 of the standard (-cpp-version  cpp11 /cpp14 ) fails with a compilation error. You see a message similar to this:  "xxx" required for copy that was eliminated, cannot be referenced -- it is a deleted function. For example, in this code snippet, the defaulted move constructor S(S&&) noexcept(false) = default  is omitted by the compiler when you run the code:   struct S {    S(int);    S(S const&);    S(S&&) noexcept(false) = default;  };  S g() {    return 42;    } The analysis fails during the compilation phase and you see this message: error: "S::S(S &&)", required for copy that was eliminated, cannot be referenced -- it is a deleted function
Workaround: A possible workaround is to remove the "= default" specifier for the constructor mentioned in the error message. In the previous example, comment out the "= default" specifier to avoid the issue:? ?S(S&&) noexcept(false) /* = default */;
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Toolstrip in the Simscape Variable Viewer is empty
Description: In a Simscape?model window, on the?Debug? tab, when you click?Simscape? >?Variable Viewer , the Variable Viewer window opens but?the buttons in its toolstrip are not visible, which makes the tool unusable.
Workaround: To work around the issue:Start a new MATLAB session. Before opening any Simscape models, issue the command simscape.state.internal.mdom_viewer(false).?For any Simscape models that you open in this session, the Variable Viewer works as expected.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail during delay balancing when your model has Discrete-Time Integrator?block with?external?rising or falling edge reset.?
Description: HDL code generation fails during delay balancing when the model has Discrete-Time Integrator?block with? external?rising or falling edge reset. The following error occurred:?  Delay balancing unsuccessful because Delay introduced in feedback loop cannot be path balanced. Offending Block:model/Subsystem/Discrete-Time Integrator.

Summary: Data type Propagation Block may result in numerical mismatch
Description: HDL Code generation from a subsystem containing Data type Propagation block may result in numerical mismatch. This is due to incorrect reference type data from the Data type propagation block in the generated model. However, the generated HDL Code is correct. This only occurs if there are no optimizations enabled in the model.?
Workaround: consider enabling any of the optimizations like Distributed pipelining, streaming/sharing, Adaptive pipelining...
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: 'For Each Subsystem' configured with vector or matrix inputs might generates an internal assertion.
Description: When your model contains a 'For each Subsystem' that has vector or matrix inputs, HDL Coder generates an internal assertion if the Partition Dimension ?of the 'For Each Subsystem' exceeds input dimension. The following assertion error occurs: ?Assertion error:B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\ForEachSubsystem.cpp:280:partitionData.dimension == 1 || partitionData.dimension == 2?
Workaround: Ensure that the Partition Dimension of the For each Subsystem must be less than or equal to the input dimensions.

Summary: Incorrect initialization of externally defined state variables with Data store memory blocks?
Description: The Remove initialization statements for externally defined state variables ?option in the Simulink Code Generation configuration does not work as expected when "Data Store Memory" is used in models that define variables as "Imported Extern" or "External Global" signals.  The bug results in externally defined state variables being initialized in the generated PLC code even when the option is activated. This occurs when the model is saved with an Underspecified Initialization option ?as "Simplified " in the Advanced Parameters of the Data Validity section of the Code Generation configuration.

Summary: Code generated for resample function with fixed-size nonuniform column vector input might cause large bss segment on Linux platform
Description: Suppose that: You generate code for the resample ?(Signal Processing Toolbox) function syntax that accepts a nonuniform input signal x : [_] = resample(x,tx,_) ? When defining input types for code generation, you specify the input signal x  to be a fixed-size column vector  In such situations, the generated code?contains large static arrays that might cause large bss segments during execution on Linux platform.
Workaround: To minimize bss segment usage, use a row vector instead of a column vector as input to the resample function.

Summary: HDL Coder generated model throws a compilation error when the initial value of the Delay block has floating-point complex input values which have an imaginary part as zero
Description: When you provide the initial value of Delay block with reset and enable via 'Input port' as a complex input of floating-point datatype, during the HDL code generation, a Constant block is created in generated model to hold this initial value. If the complex value has imaginary part as 0, then generated model throws a compilation error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from Bit-wise AND operation between signed integers might be incorrect
Description: The generated code might be incorrect code for a MATLAB function or Simulink model that meets the following conditions: The Casting mode ?parameter is set ?to?Standards Compliant . The Bit-wise AND operation is performed between signed integers using the Bitand function or the Bitwise block.  In the generated code, the signed integer is converted to an unsigned type before performing the Bit-wise operation, which is incorrect.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code generation result for Truth Table block that directly connects to a conditionally executed subsystem Outport block
Description: The initial value of the Truth Table block output is overwritten by the Outport block when all these conditions apply: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled or triggered subsystem. The subsystem contains a Truth Table block. An output of the Truth Table block directly connects to an Outport block of the subsystem. The parameter Initial value ?of the Truth Table block output port has a different value than the parameter Initial output ?of the Outport block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Truth Table block and the Outport block.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: nargin(fun)?returns negative value in generated code if fun uses both?varargin?and arguments validation block
Description: The generated code returns a negative value for the nargin(fun)  syntax if:? The function  fun ?contains an?arguments ?validation block? One of the inputs of fun ?is ?varargin .?  In this situation, MATLAB execution returns a positive value.?  This issue does not affect calls to?nargin ?without an input.  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, and MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink.

Summary: Full-duplex audio can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Full-duplex audio can crash MATLAB if audioDeviceReader  is set up before audioDeviceWriter , or when an overrun or underrun occurs, in particular with devices that have more than four channels. This bug affects audioPlayerRecorder , or the simultaneous use of audioDeviceWriter  and audioDeviceReader  with the same device (with Driver  set to 'ASIO'  if using Windows).
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0702?displays an incorrect warning for literal '1'
Description: Model Advisor check Check usage of numeric literals in Stateflow ?(mathworks.jmaab.jc_0702 ) displays below incorrect warning when type casted '1'  is used in increment operations in MATLAB Action language charts. This warning is incorrect as this is an exception to the guideline. Warning  The following expressions contain numeric literals:?  Block Path??????? Expression  Chart/StateA???? TimerValue= TimerValue + uint8(1);

Summary: Read-only property on subsystem block is not respected for Subsystem Reference instance.
Description: Setting the read-only property on a Subsystem Reference instance does not get respected. The subsystem graph is not locked which allows the subsystem graph content to be modified.

Summary: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing Simulink function or Graphical function in Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash or produce an error when generating code for a model containing a Stateflow chart under the following circumstances: The Stateflow chart has a Simulink function exported from Stateflow. The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function.  When generating code, you may get this code generation error: ValidCore violated: Failed assertion: fcnType->numInputs() == nInputs || (fcnType->isVariadic() && fcnType->numInputs()".   MATLAB might crash. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:[ 0] 0x00007ff8bd15fd64 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+06356324 RTWCG::RTWContext::getModule+00000020  [ 1] 0x00007ff8bcc5924a C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\rtwcg.dll+01086026 CG::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>::GenericTagCategory<CG::RTWCGMatlabFcnInplaceTag,0>+00000714
Workaround: For Simulink function, set the Function Packaging parameter to Inline and for Graphical function, set the Function Inline option to?Inline.?
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 3, R2023b

Summary: Code Prover analysis stuck at?Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 22.1 (P_IIP)
Description: When your code contains floating point conversions, in certain cases, the analysis might get stuck at Software Safety Analysis Level 0. The issue happens because the pointer analysis faces a convergence problem when trying to propagate pointer values via floating point conversions. The issue might be visible by default on small applications where the analysis invokes a precise pointer analysis mode. For larger applications, the issue is seen only when using the option -improve-pointer-analysis-precision .

Summary: Custom attributes are omitted when importing requirements with custom URIs from IBM DOORS Next?
Description: When you import requirements from IBM DOORS Next, requirements' artifact attributes are imported as custom attributes. However, artifact attributes that satisfy one of these criteria are omitted during import:The attribute has a custom URI. The attribute is based on an attribute data type that has a custom URI.? The attribute is based on an enumeration based attribute data type that has an enumeration value that has a custom URI.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 5, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Simulink.Parameter with expression value has a different behavior than Simulink.Parameter with numeric value in mask initialization
Description: When you use aSimulink.Parameter  object as the value of a mask parameter, if the value of the parameter object is an expression, the behavior of the initialization code is different than if the value of the parameter object is a numeric value. When the value of the parameter object is an expression the parameter is not restored as a Simulink.Parameter  object after exiting the initialization code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Generated model does not reflect optimizations for masked Library or Subsystem.
Description: When you enable optimizations for your design that contains masked Library or masked Subsystem and generate a model. The generated model is incorrect as it does not reflect optimizations for? Masked Library or Masked Subsystem. This?may result in numerical mismatch. However, thegenerated HDL code is correct.
Workaround: Consider disabling optimizations or if optimizations are required, consider disabling?Model Generation in the?Model Configuration Parameters > Global Settings > Model Generation > Generated Model.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022b Update 5, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using VISA-related functionality on Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x
Description: MATLAB might crash on Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. Impacted MATLAB releases install NI-VISA 21.0 with this support package. The crash can occur when using any of the following VISA-related functions, apps, or Simulink blocks: visa  visadev  visadevlist  instrhwinfo  Test and Measurement Tool Query Instrument?block To Instrument?block  For example, the following line of code crashes MATLAB: instrhwinfo("visa","ni")
Workaround: To work around this issue, install the NI-VISA 2022 Q4 driver on your Intel-based macOS Monterey 12.x machine.Download and install NI-VISA 2022 Q4 by following the instructions on the NI-VISA Download website.

Summary: Observer configuration not saved if observer is in internal test harness
Description: If an observer is in an internal test harness, any updates to the observer, such as remapping signals, are not saved.
Workaround: Convert the internal test harness to an external test harness.

Summary: Setting "Synthesis Tool" option to "Microchip Libero SoC" in HDL Coder Configuration Parameters window leads to an error
Description: Setting the "Synthesis Tool" option in the HDL Coder Configuration Parameter window to "Microchip Libero SoC" leads to error:?Index exceeds the number of array elements  .  This error does not occur when setting the "Synthesis Tool" to any other value.
Workaround: Use the command line workflow to set the parameter.hdlset_param(<modelname>,'SynthesisTool', 'Microchip Libero SoC')?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant?Bus Element Out without signal names may generate incorrect code when output is driven from a subsystem
Description: A Simulink model containing conditional?Bus Element Out ? may generate incorrect code when the below conditions are true: The variant condition on the Bus Element Out  is propagated through a Variant Source  or Variant Sink . The Bus Element out  does not have a signal name assigned. The model contains Bus Creator  and Bus Selector  blocks for routing the conditional bus signals. The output is driven from an atomic subsystem or a virtual subsystem with Treat as grouped when propagating variant conditions  parameter is turned on  In the generated code, you can notice the output (e.g.:?rtb_VM_Conditional_Signal_Out1_BusCreator_0 ) is incorrectly reset to zero.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

Summary: Details dialog not showing for enumeration types in Interface Editor
Description: When selecting an enumerated type in the spreadsheet in the Interface Editor, the Details dialog on the right hand side does not render, therefore the enumeration cannot be configured.
Workaround: Use the Interface Dictionary API to configure enumerated types.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The model parameter Allow clock-rate pipelining of DUT output ports can result in inconsistent I/O between the original model and HDL code
Description: When using the model parameter?ClockRatePipelineOutputPorts , the ordering of the outputs in the generated model and HDL code may not be the same as what was modeled originally. If these outputs have differing amounts of data rate latency, the validation model can show a mismatch.  Additionally, if input signals inside the DUT are left unconnected or drive unconnected logic that is considered dead and removed by optimizations, the signals are missing from the generated model and HDL code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant block in referenced model driving conditionally executed subsystem output might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions apply:The?Underspecified initialization detection  configuration parameter is set to?Simplified ?for the model. The model contains a Model block inside a triggered or function-call subsystem whose trigger port is unconnected or connected to a ground block. The output of the Model block connects directly to an Outport block in the conditionally executed subsystem. The output of the Model block is driven by a Constant block connected to a root Outport block in the referenced model. The?Sample time ?of the Constant block is set to?-1 .
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Model block output port and the Outport block in the unconnected triggered or function-call subsystem.

Summary: AUTOSAR-C++14 A0-1-6 false positive on public unused alias types in a class template
Description: Polyspace BugFinder reports a false positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A0-1-6 on?public  unused alias types in a class template. These aliases might be declared to be compatible with STL functions.

Summary: Input Method Editors do not appear in the correct place in the MATLAB Editor on Windows
Description: On Windows, when using the MATLAB Editor in non-English locales, such as Japanese or Chinese, the Input Method Editors (IMEs) do not appear in the correct location. They should appear near the current text cursor position, but instead appear in the upper-left corner of the monitor.
