Summary: Log axes limits might jump after an interaction
Description: This issue occurs with the following conditions: The figure was created using the uifigure  function or is a figure in MATLAB Online. At least one axis has log scale. Axes limits were set programmatically before the interaction. The plot uses "serverside rendering", which is a configuration that can be used in some apps such as the SerDes Designer.  For example, consider the following MATLAB code. In the following example, serverside rendering is explicitly turned on. fig = uifigure;fig.getCanvas.ServerSideRendering = 'on';ax = axes('Parent',fig);xlim(ax, [1 3]);ax.XScale = 'log';% Interactively pan the axesxlim(ax, [6 7])% Interactively pan the axes. The axes limits jump to the original limits after the interaction.

Summary: SIL simulation with coverage enabled causes an error in the diagnostics viewer
Description: SIL simulation with coverage enabled causes the following error when the model has custom code or model blocks in SIL mode: Unexpected error during coverage extraction for code view:Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: Data Store Read or Write blocks using?enumerated?data types?in Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function blocks might produce different SIL/PIL and normal mode simulation results
Description: Results from software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) and normal mode simulations might differ numerically if all of the following are true: A model component has Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks.  The Data Store Read?or Data Store Write blocks use an?enumerated  data? type that is not based on the built-in?int32 ?data type .  The Data Store Read?or Data Store Write?blocks?are located in Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Funtion blocks.
Workaround: For the Data Store Read?or Data Store Write blocks?in Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Funtion blocks, use?enumerated?data types that are based on?int32.

Summary: buildinfo.mat?error when MdlrefConfigMismatch check?is run with?parallel building of referenced models enabled
Description: When you run the Simulink Coder Model Advisor check,? mathworks.codegen.MdlrefConfigMismatch,  with parallel building of referenced models enabled ( EnableParallelModelReferenceBuilds  set to? 'on' ), the check produces an error indicating that it is unable to find? buildInfo.mat .
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Polyspace plugin for Visual Studio does not show findings
Description: The Polyspace as You Code plugin for Visual Studio does not display findings found by the Polyspace engine.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when initial conditions of a Stateflow chart are reset by the surrounding subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model containing a subsystem that meets the following conditions: The subsystem contains a Stateflow chart that has initial values for states and data.? The subsystem also contains one of these control port blocks:? Action Port block with the parameter States when execution is resumed ?is set to reset .? Enable Port block with the parameter States when enabling  is set to reset .? Trigger block with the parameter States when enabling ?is set to reset .? Reset block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Numerical mismatch between normal and rapid accelerator simulations involving nested conditionally executed subsystems and Bus Selector blocks
Description: Numerical mismatch between normal and rapid accelerator simulations might occur for a model that meets all  of these conditions:The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem with two Outport blocks that pass identical signals. The subsystem is nested inside another conditionally executed subsystem that has two Outport blocks. The Outport blocks of the outer subsystem have the block parameter Initial output  set to []  (empty matrix). The two outputs of the inner subsystems are connected to the Outport blocks of the outer subsystem through two Bus Selector blocks. The Bus Selector blocks have identical attributes in terms of data type, dimensions, and complexity. One Bus Selector block directly connects Outport blocks of the inner and outer Subsystems. The other Bus Selector block is in an Atomic subsystem and connects Outport blocks of the inner and outer subsystems across the atomic subsystem boundaries.  Because the Initial output  parameter of the outer Outport blocks is set to?[] ?(empty matrix), the blocks attempt to inherit their initial values from the input signals. An Atomic subsystem is not a valid source of initial values, therefore one outer Outport block?uses  the default initial value of the output data type?where the other one inherits the initial value from the inner conditionally executed subsystem. Because of the difference between the initial values, the outport block signal buffers are not reused. However, Simulink erroneously reuses the two buffers in rapid accelerator simulation mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: String elements in output bus signals from conditionally executed subsystems might be incorrect in simulation
Description: When a conditionally executed subsystem has bus outputs with string elements, simulation results might be incorrect when all of the following conditions are true: The subsystem is configured to reset output values when disabled. The subsystem has more than one output of the same bus type with string elements. Those bus outputs all have only partial initial conditions, where the value of parameter "Initial output" is a MATLAB structure that does not have all fields of the bus type. For example, the bus type has 3 elements a , b ,  and c , while the parameter value is a structure with only two fields,?a  and b . The model runs in normal or accelerated mode.
Workaround: Consider using one of these workarounds:Run model in SIL or rapid accelerator mode.Do not use partial initialization in "Initial output" parameter of Outport blocks.Use uint8 vectors instead of strings in the output bus signals.

Summary: MATLAB may crash due to memory violation in masked blocks having port constraints.
Description: Memory violation may occur while editing masked blocks having port constraints. This may lead to MATLAB crash.  The stack trace typically have the following frames:? Segmentation violation [0] SLMaskContext::getConstPortConstraint(int) const [1] SLMaskNS::MaskParameterConditionContext::getConstParameterCondition() const [2] SLMaskNS::ParameterConditionHandle::getParameterName()) const

Summary: Simulink Coverage qualification test report can include false negative results for out-of-scope parameters
Description: Some Simulink Coverage parameters are not relevant for model coverage testing. When executing the qualification test procedure, comparison of the actual results against the expected results for these parameters can return an incorrect false negative.?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Performance regression during pathmetrics object creation while using a map with a state validator object
Description: A performance regression occurs during pathmetrics  object creation while using a map with a state validator object. The regression is noticeable when creating the object iteratively, as demonstrated by the following code: % Load a map and create a state validator object for the occupancy map.load exampleMaps.matmapObj = occupancyMap(complexMap);validatorObj = validatorOccupancyMap(stateSpaceSE2);validatorObj.Map = mapObj;validatorObj.ValidationDistance = 0.1;% Precomputed states from plannerRRT for the specified map.states = [20.0000 20.0000 0;????????? 27.0000 20.0000 0;?? ???? ? 34.0000 20.0000 0;?? ???? ? 41.0000 20.0000 0;?? ???? ? 48.0000 20.0000 0;?? ???? ? 52.0000 20.0000 0;?? ???? ? 58.9851 19.5476 -0.0666;?? ???? ? 67.0899 19.0073 -0.0666;?? ???? ? 75.1948 18.4670 -0.0666;?? ???? ? 83.2917 17.8593 -0.2170;?? ???? ? 88.0000 12.0000 -1.5708];% Create a navPath object from the precomputed states.stateSpace = stateSpaceSE2([0 300; 0 200; -pi pi]);pthObj = navPath(stateSpace,states);% Create a pathmetrics object 1000 times in a loop and observe the execution time using the tic and toc functions.tic;for i = 1:1000??? pathmetricsObj = pathmetrics(pthObj,validatorObj);endtoc;

Summary: Wrong line indicated in debugger for a loop or conditional branch in a MATLAB function
Description: While debugging a standalone MATLAB Function block or MATLAB function in a Stateflow chart, MATLAB might indicate that the debugger stops at the wrong line of code when stepping over a loop or a conditional branch. This bug occurs when the following conditions are true: The loop or conditional branch is the last code section in the function The condition controlling the loop or branch is false.?
Workaround: Add a no-op line of code after the conditional or loop branch like below.function myFcn1?if (<condition>)?? ? ? ?<statement>???end? temp = 1;end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Symbolic dimension is incorrectly inlined during model reference code generation
Description: The code generated for a model reference hierarchy might incorrectly inline the symbolic dimension variable for a signal under these conditions:The signal crosses model reference boundaries. One of the parent models in the model reference hierarchy is not connected to the data source (base workspace, Simulink data dictionary, or external source) used by the referenced model.?
Workaround: Explicitly link the parent model(s) to the data source, even if the objects in the source are not directly used by the model.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing Stateflow Charts that have conditionally executed?while?or?do-while?constructs might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow Chart and a While Iterator block. The?Function packaging ?parameter of the Chart is set to Auto  or Inline.  The Chart also contains a conditionally executed while  or do-while  construct.  In the generated code, a part of the?while ?or do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted.? For example, the correct code contains this while  loop that contains a do-while  loop while() {?  ? ? a =? inMatrix[1];  ? ? do {?  ? ? ? ? a++;?  ? ? ? ? // do something else  ? ? } while();  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += a? ? ?  ? ? }? }? In the incor rect code, the do-while  loop is incorrectly hoisted, and the outer while  loop becomes: do {  ? ? // do something else?  ?} while();?  while() {?  ? ? temp = inMatrix[1];?  ? ? for(i=...) {?  ? ? ? ? outMatrix[i] += temp ?} }?
Workaround: Set the?Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to Reusable function or Nonreusable function.

Summary: Generating IP core for?Generic Xilinx Platform?in HDL Workflow Advisor may fail when the Allow clock-rate pipelining for DUT output ports settings is enabled for a model
Description: Using the IP Core generation workflow for Generic Xilinx Platform , HDL Workflow Advisor task 3.2 Generate RTL code and IP core ?fails if the configuration parameters Allow clock-rate pipelining for DUT output ports  is enabled for the model. The following error is generated:? Error using hdlcoder.pir/getDutExtraLatency  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.   Contact support@mathworks.com with reproduction steps.   Assertion Failed With Debug Message:   B:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\delaydata.cpp:405:outPort->isData()
Workaround: Disable Allow Clock-rate Pipelining for DUT output ports in the configuration parameters.

Summary: Code generation error when code interface packaging is C++ Class and system target file is custom derived from ert.tlc
Description: C++ code generation might produce an error for a model with the configuration parameter CodeInterfacePackaging ?set to C++ Class? when the model uses a custom target file derived from ert.tlc. The error assertion might be similar to either of the following: Code generation assertion 'fpcArgSpecType != FPC_Value' failed in..  STD exception 'std::logic_error': 'basic_string::_M_construct null not valid' was caught.
Workaround: Review? the instructions to "Support C Function Prototype Control" for custom embedded targets and apply the steps found in section "Declaring C Function Prototype Control Compliance".
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect rapid accelerator mode simulation results for models using root-level bus element ports and function-call input ports
Description: Simulating a model results in incorrect answers when all ?of these conditions apply: The model simulates in rapid accelerator mode. The model has at least one In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block at the root level. The model has at least one Inport block at the root level that outputs a function-call signal and has a sample time of -1 . The ExternalInput  configuration parameter specifies time steps when the function-call input will run.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory corruption of?imported enumerations of size smaller than native integer
Description: When a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block writes to an output data of an imported enumerated type with size smaller than native integer, memory corruption occurs during simulation. This might lead to incorrect simulation results and a mismatch between simulation and generated code.
Workaround: Use enumerations of the same size as native integer by inheriting from Simulink.IntEnumType.

Summary: Negative latency cycle counts for LSTM layers in the profiler log?
Description: The profiler incorrectly assumes that frame N+1 will not start until frame N is completed leads to negative latency cycle counts for LSTM layers in the profiler log. The scheduler used to assign profiler events to a frame number interleaves computations between frames instead .??

Summary: Scrollbar might not appear in tables created using the uitable function when the Data property is set
Description: When setting Data  increases the number of columns in a table created using the uitable  function, the horizontal scrollbar might fail to appear. tbl = uitable(uifigure);tbl.RowName = '';tbl.ColumnName = '';drawnow% Set Data property; increase number of columns in tabledata = ["-0.027" "-0.037" "0.01" "0.024" "0.031" "0.007" "0.026" "0.017" "0.01" "0.026" "0.014" ...        "0.021" "0.776" "7.41" "5.851" "0.0013274" "1.009" "-1.125" "4.769" "2.12" "5.29"];tbl.Data = data;
Workaround: To recover the horizontal scrollbar, set the RowName property to a different value and then back to its previous value after setting the Data property.tbl = uitable(uifigure);tbl.RowName = '';tbl.ColumnName = '';drawnow% Set Data property; increase number of columns in tabledata = ["-0.027" "-0.037" "0.01" "0.024" "0.031" "0.007" "0.026" "0.017" "0.01" "0.026" "0.014" ...        "0.021" "0.776" "7.41" "5.851" "0.0013274" "1.009" "-1.125" "4.769" "2.12" "5.29"];tbl.Data = data;drawnow% Workaroundtbl.RowName = 'numbered';drawnowtbl.RowName = '';

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys
Description: MATLAB might crash when you select and move signal lines in a Simulink model multiple times using the arrow keys. Moving signal lines does not include changing connections. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to the following: [ 0] 0x00007ffd4ab671cb Simulink::Routing::getPartialSegment[ 1] 0x00007ffd4ab7f598 `anonymous namespace'::getPartialSegmentKeyPairs?
Workaround: When moving signal lines using arrow keys, reselect the lines after each move or drag them using the pointer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects causes a degradation in Model Explorer performance
Description: Having a large number of Data Dictionary objects can negatively affect the performance of the Model Explorer to the point that the Model Explorer becomes unresponsive.

Summary: Accelerator mode simulation of an export function model with root-level Simulink function generates an incorrect result
Description: Accelerator mode simulation generates incorrect result for an export function model if all the following conditions are met: The model contains a Simulink function at the root level. The Simulink function is called from another export function within the model. The Simulink function contains a nested function-call subsystem.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: polyspace-configure?cannot extract predefined macro definitions of qcc compiler
Description: polyspace-configure  can extract the predefined macro definitions of the gcc  compiler by passing option -dM  to that compiler (gcc -dM  lists all the gcc  predefined macros). Because qcc  is a gcc  based compiler, Polyspace attempts to use the same option to obtain the qcc  macro definition. However, because qcc  uses a slightly different option to list its predefined macros, polyspace-configure  cannot extract the qcc  macro definitions and you see a warning message similar to this in the log: WARNING: The predefined macros could not be computed and it may result in an incomplete project  If Polyspace does not know the definitions of the compiler macros, the analysis might fail.
Workaround: Use the option -import-macro-definitions from-source-tokens when you run polyspace-configure.The option changes the strategy that?polyspace-configure?uses to compute the predefined macros for only the current run.

Summary: System object blocks are not considered when verifying unsupported blocks check using HDL Model Advisor
Description: The HDL Model Advisor does not consider system object blocks when verifying unsupported blocks during the unsupported block check.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystem outport with wide signal having a Source block with constant sample time might result in incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all these conditions: The DefaultParameterBehavior ?configuration parameter is set to Inlined . The?BufferReusableBoundary  c onfiguration parameter is selected. The model contains a reusable subsystem whose outport has a muxed signal. The muxed signal at the reusable subsystem?outport has a source with constant sample time and  a source with discrete or asynchronous sample time
Workaround: Manually insert a Signal Conversion block inside the reusable subsystem at the outport.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code returns incorrect results?for deep learning input layers with normalization with?StandardDeviation set?to 0
IP core Generation workflow task 2.1?Check Model Settings?in HDL Workflow Advisor fails when the?ProdHWDeviceType?property is disabled for model.
Description: Using the IP Core generation workflow in HDL Workflow Advisor, task 2.1 Check Model Settings ?fails if the below conditions are true: The hardware board is set in the model configuration parameters under Hardware Implementation > Hardware Board  The?ProdHWDeviceType ?property is disabled for the model  The following error occurs:setCheckResultStatus method can only be called while executing check callback.
Workaround: Change the Hardware Board settings to 'None' in the model configuration parameters and Enable the "ProdHWDeviceType" property in the Simulink model

Summary: MATLAB might crash when updating a model with a Data Store Read or Data Store Write block dialog box open
Description: Updating a Simulink model causes MATLAB to crash when both these conditions are true: The model contains a Data Store Read or Data Store Write block that reads or writes to a Simulink.Signal  object with a Simulink.Bus  object data type. The dialog box for the Data Store Read or Data Store Write block is open.

Summary: MATLAB scripts generated by the Design Filter Live Editor task may not update correctly when filter parameter values are changed in quick succession
Description: Setting two or more filter parameters back-to-back in quick succession may cause some of the parameters to not be updated in the MATLAB code generated by the Design Filter  Live Editor task.

Summary: Datasmith export is missing collisions in Unreal Engine.
Description: When importing a Datasmith file exported from RoadRunner into Unreal Engine, the resulting scene does not have collisions.
Workaround: Select all the static meshes that were imported into Unreal Engine.Right click the selected meshes. Select Asset Actions. Under Asset Actions, select Bulk Edit via Property Matrix...Search for the Collision Complexity property.Change it to "Use Complex Collision As Simple".

Summary: Model Advisor check "Signal name propagation"?incorrectly flags Stateflow chart block when Look Under Mask?is set to all.
Description: Model Advisor check "Signal name propagation "  (id:  mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009 ) ?i ncorrectly flags Stateflow charts when the subcheck for input parameter ?Look Under Mask ?is set to all .?
Workaround: Exclude chart block from analysis for check "Signal name propagation"?(id:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0009)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink incorrectly reuses memory for persistent data of type identical to a Simulink bus?in a MATLAB Function Block
Description: Code generated from a model that meets all of the following conditions might produce incorrect results: The model uses at least two instances of a MATLAB?Function Block from a library. The block has a persistent variable of bus type that has at least one variable-size field. The inferred bus type of the persistent variable is identical to a bus defined in the Type Editor (Bus Editor). For example, this can be achieved by assigning MATLAB Function input of bus type to the persistent variable at initialization. Two instances write different values to the persistent variable.?  The two instances might incorrectly use the same memory for their persistent variable.

Summary: Multitasking analysis might fail when you redefine void type with typedef
Description: If you use typedef  to declare an alias for void  type, for instance: ?typedef void void_t;   Polyspace might not recognize functions of type? void_t  in your multitasking code and the analysis might fail. You see an error message similar to this:? Error in parameter -interrupts: interrupt has non-void prototype   For example, if you define a function isr ?in this way: typedef void void_t;?void_t isr(void_t)? {...}??   and you then use the option -interrupts  to specify that the function isr ?in an interrupt (-interrupts isr ), Polyspace does not consider that isr  has type void and the analysis fails.

Summary: Unused ports of PS-Simulink Converter block in generated HDL implementation model might cause error during HDL code generation
Description: Any unused output ports of PS-Simulink Converter block in generated HDL implementation model might lead to an error or incorrect result during HDL code generation.
Workaround: Terminate all floating/unused PS-S ports of simscape network or in the generated implementation model.

Summary: Creating a Simulink behavior from software components with client or server ports might produce error
Description: When you create a Simulink behavior from a software component in a software architecture, the model might produce an error if the following conditions are true: The component has client or server ports.? You change the name of the input or output ports.? The name is already associated with the default name of another port after conversion. For example, if you have two input ports, you change the name of the second input port to Function Element Call1  which is the default name for the first input port after creating the Simulink behavior.  If the above conditions are met, you might observe the following error: The name 'Function Element Call1' already exists in 'Component'.?
Workaround: You can change the name of the input or output ports back to the default value, or use a name different than the default names of the ports after creating the Simulink behavior.

Summary: Interacting with aggregated coverage results from subsystem harnesses run with fast restart iterations might error
Description: After simulating multiple harnesses for different subsystems in a model using fast restart iterations in the Simulink Test Manager, an error might occur when interacting with the aggregated coverage results. For example, when attempting to generate a coverage report for these aggregated results, you might see an assertion failure similar to:? failed assertion in B:\ matlab\ toolbox\ slcoverage\ src\ slvnv\ utils\ object. cpp at line 2471:? id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL?

Summary: Model containing Constantblocks with overflowing fixed-point values might cause simulation mismatch?
Description: Simulation mismatch may occur in the generatedmodel when your model contains Constant block with a fixed-point data type and thefixed-point value exceeds the largest value that can be precisely representedusing double datatype.?

Summary: Logged signals not shown for tests using block diagram harness and parameter overrides if model dirtied during compilation
Description: A test that uses a block diagram harness and parameter overrides does not show the logged signals in the Test Manager if the model is dirtied by a callback during compilation, such as by an InitFcn  on a mask.
Workaround: Set the Dirty flag of the model to off in the postload callback of the test case:?set_param('<model_name>','Dirty','off');
Fixed In: R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 3, R2023a

The simulation mode of the model is set to accelerator mode.  The crash might trigger an assertion or it might report a std::terminate() . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect prediction results can be created for sequential networks (those including one or more lstmLayers).
Description: For sequential networks that include? lstmLayers, incorrect predict results can be created. This issue typically occurs in networks with a large number of hidden state elements.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When generating HDL code from MATLAB code with the MATLAB Datapath workflow, division operations between fixed-point values and constants may generate incorrect code
Description: Division operations may generate incorrect code when  generating HDL code from either of these options. A MATLAB function block that has? the HDL Block Property Architecture  set to?MATLAB Datapath  A MATLAB function with the Aggressive Dataflow Conversion ?parameter enabled in Optimizations? ?tab of the HDL Code Generation ?task in?the MATLAB HDL Workflow Advisor  In either case, division operations may generate incorrect code when both of these conditions are true. One operand of the division expression is a fixed-point value The other operand is a non-fixed-point constant value  For example, operations such as?x/0.5  and 2.1/x  may generate incorrect code if x  is a fixed-point value.
Workaround: To avoid generating incorrect code, follow one of these options:Cast the constant value to a fixed-point type, instead of using a double data type for the constant value. If the constant is on the right hand side of the division operation, consider multiplying by the inverse of the constant instead of dividing. For example, instead of x/0.5?rewrite the expression to be?x*2.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during GPU code generation when?the long long data type is not supported by the selected hardware
Description: GPU code generation requires the long long data type and tries to set the target long long mode to be true. But if target long long mode cannot be set to be true or production hardware is used for code generation (cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdEqTarget =?true ), MATLAB might crash and throw the following error messages: In validateNumBits: Cannot emit constant with too many bits.?  In approximateRank: could not determine what integer type to produce.? Request was for 64 bits, but long long has 32 bits.?
Workaround: Set the following property of the codegen configuration object to avoid the crash,cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdLongLongMode = true;If?ProdLongLongMode is immutable, you can generate code on the target (test) hardware to emulate the behavior of the production hardware by setting,cfg.HardwareImplementation.ProdEqTarget = false;Note this approach only works when the target hardware supports the long long data type.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator might not correctly generate access function calls
Description: The code generator does not correctly generate access function calls when all ?of the following are true: The model contains a referenced model inside an iterator subsystem. The inputs and outputs of the iterator subsystem are mapped to signals that have the GetSet  access function storage class.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a, R2023b

Summary: Internal assertion is generated on modeling the RAM blocks without clock-enable
Description: An internal assertion is generated during the HDL code generation when your model consisting of RAM blocks is configured with the following settings:? 'RAMArchitecture' ?parameter is set to 'WithoutClockEnable' .? Input signal to RAM blocks has Inf  sample time.?  The following internal assertion is generated :?  Assertion Failed With Debug Message:pir_backend/ RamLowering. cpp:147:retval >= 0?
Workaround: Either propagate discrete sample rates or set 'RAMArchitecture' to 'WithClockEnable'.

Summary: Build process might fail if models use C Caller blocks to interface external functions that use formal parameters of type pointer to matrix
Description: You may get build errors or warnings while building the C/C++ code generated from a model that meets these conditions: The model uses a C Caller block to integrate external functions. The functions use formal parameters of type pointer that point to matrix variables.??  Consider that a C Caller block integrates the following external function: typedef double myType[5];  double foo(const myType* in);  While building the generated, the code generator throws this error/ warning : cannot convert 'const real_T*' {aka 'const double*'} to 'const double (*)[5]'

Summary: Live scripts, visualizations, and UIs disappear on Macintosh computers when configuring displays
Description: Connecting or disconnecting an external monitor, or changing the screen resolution of a Macintosh computer might cause problems with displayed content in a running MATLAB session. Specifically, these aspects of the user interface might be affected: Live Editor content Figures created with the uifigure  function SimBiology Model Analyzer Other visualizations or UIs

Summary: Change tracking, implementation and verification status degrade Requirements Editor performance
Description: When you display change tracking, implementation status, or verification status and you have a large number of requirements and requirement links in memory, you may experience a significant impact on the performance of operations like: Opening new requirement files Closing requirement files Creating or deleting links Opening the requirements perspective Opening models with the requirements perspective active  The performance impact will increase as more requirements and links are loaded into memory.?
Workaround: You can avoid the performance impact by disabling change tracking, implementation, and verification status from the user interface.? However you must disable these settings from the Requirements Editor?and Requirements Perspective in every open model. Change tracking is enabled by default so this issue can be experienced with the standard configuration.? For more information about how to disable this information please visit the following documentation pages:Track Changes to Requirements LinksReview Requirements Implementation StatusReview Requirements Verification StatusFor convenience, a utility script attached below can simplify the workaround process by invoking the necessary configuration changes across all windows.? The utility also provides the ability to adjust user stored preferences so that impacts are avoided when opening models that are configured with requirements perspective active.Consider invoking this script as part of any environment setup automation to reduce the likelihood of experiencing issues.

Summary: The Property Inspector does not update after using functions in the Command Window that modify the selected element
Description: The Property Inspector  shows the current properties of the selected element in the editor such as a component, port, connector, interface, or function. If you make a modification to the selected element from MATLAB Command Window using existing programmatic interfaces, the Property Inspector  does not automatically update. You must select elsewhere and then re-select the element to see the updated property.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails when?the model contains a root-level outport driven by an inactive state owner
Description: When a model contains a root-level outport driven by a state owner block that is located within an inactive variant subsystem, then the compatibility check fails with the following error message: Building model representation failed: The Outport block <block_path> has the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' turned on but Simulink cannot guarantee that the outport will be virtual in generated code due to the following reasons. One of the ways to correct this issue is to set the parameter 'Ensure outport is virtual' to 'off'.?  The error message states to set the parameter?Ensure outport is virtual  ?to?off  ? even? when the parameter is cleared.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 4, R2023a

Summary: Internal Exception thrown by Simulink Design Verifier during compatibility analysis.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier throws an internal exception during compatibility analysis for a model in the case where arrays of enumerations inheriting from built-in types like int8  are used with arrays of enumerations inheriting from Simulink.IntEnumType  as a part of the bus as inputs. Error message:? Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: <Model> is incompatible for test generation with Simulink Design Verifier

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect C code generated when function-call subsystem writes to its output conditionally.
Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code when a parameter or signal is mapped to an access function that returns a pointer
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code if all ?of the following conditions are met:? The model contains a signal or parameter that is mapped to an access function that returns a pointer The signal or parameter is shared between a Simulink built-in block and a user-created S-Function block The S-Function block inlines its code using TLC

Summary: Inactive branch of if-action subsystem might run unexpectedly
Description: The inactive branch of if-action subsystems might run unexpectedly when all of the following conditions are true: Simulation runs in Normal mode. The subsystem associated with the inactive branch is multirate. The output of the subsystem has continuous sample time. The model is configured to use a fixed-step solver and multitasking.

Summary: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for a model that uses standards compliant casting
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code when all ?of the following are true:? The model uses any of the following storage classes or access functions on the I/O of a referenced Model block or S-Function that uses TLC for code generation: A?GetSet  storage class. A user-defined storage class in an Embedded Coder Dictionary whose data access is Function.  A user-defined TLC storage class that uses different legacy APIs to get and set the data.  The Casting modes ?model configuration parameter is set?to Standards Compliant .  Embedded Coder might insert a void  cast before an access function call, leading to uncompilable C/C++ code.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running automatic float to fixed conversion with unsupported code
Description: A crash may occur when float to fixed conversion is run consecutively on a design that contains unsupported constructs. List of supported language features are listed at?https://www.mathworks.com/help/fixedpoint/ug/matlab-language-features-supported-for-automated-fixed-point-conversion.html. An example of an unsupported construct in float to fixed conversion is a?function argument validation ?block that specifies input validation. The top of the crash log would point to the?emlrtLocationLoggingPushLogInternal?function.
Workaround: If you get errors during float to fixed conversion for unsupported constructs, modify your design to remove unsupported constructs before trying float to fixed conversion again.

Summary: Code generation might take excessive time if MATLAB code uses a collision geometry with a matrix of size greater than or equal to UINT16_MAX (65535)
Description: Code generation might take excessive time or hang if both ?these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code uses Robotics Toolbox to add a collision geometry to a rigid body The collision mesh of the rigid body contains at least 65535 elements

Summary: Error during the data generation phase of Simulink Design Verifier Analysis when using tunable parameters of built-in enumeration type.
Description: The data generation phase of Simulink Design Verifier will error out if the model has a parameter marked as tunable for Simulink Design Verifier analysis and it is an enumeration which sub-classes a built-in datatype like int8   The error message will be of the following form: Error while generating data due to the following issue: Cannot find a member of the <Enumeration Class> enumeration class that corresponds to each element of the given input argument.

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6 with static local variable
Description: Polyspace incorrectly raises a false positive violation of rule MISRA-C:2012 18.6 when the address of a static local variable is returned by a function.? For example: int *foo1(int *obj) {    static int *p;     p = obj;   // False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 18.6    return p; } int main(void) {  static int *p1;   int *p2;    p2 = foo1(&p1); }

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sharing or streaming with clock-rate pipelining can cause simulation failure if the design latency is larger than the oversampling factor
Description: A Modelsim simulation failure can occur if all of these conditions are met: The sharing factor or streaming factor on a subsystem in the DUT is set to a value greater than 1 Clock-rate pipelining is enabled The design latency is larger than the oversampling factor

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a Merge block and a Unit Delay block or signals specified to have a Reusable custom storage class
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets either of the following scenarios: Scenario 1: The model contains a Merge block and a Unit Delay block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystems are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is connected to a Unit Delay block and then fed back to the input(s) of any of the conditionally executed subsystems. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.  Scenario 2: The model contains a Merge block. The outputs of a set of conditionally executed subsystem are feeding into a Merge block. The output of the Merge block is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class. The input of any of these subsystems is specified to have a Reusable custom storage class with the same variable name as the output of the Merge block. The input(s) of the conditionally executed subsystems is/are used in a computation generating an output different from the one connected to the Merge block. The execution of that output happens after the execution of the output connected to the Merge block.?
Workaround: Connect a Signal Conversion block to the output of the Merge block. In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter.
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 2, R2023a

Summary: AUTOSAR Component Quick Start always changes the model system target file to either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc.
Description: When configuring a model using the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start, the system target file automatically changes to the default system target file based on the selected platform once the wizard has finished. The system target file the model was using is replaced with either autosar.tlc or autosar_adaptive.tlc, even if the model target was already AUTOSAR compliant.
Workaround: Manually change the system target file after the AUTOSAR Component Quick Start has finished.

Summary: The keyword 'INOUT' is not generated at the DUT level in the HDL code when a model is configured with?ScalarizePorts property?'on' and?has a Black Box subsystem with?BidirectionalPort?enabled
Description: When your model consists of a Black box subsystem with HDL block property?BidirectionalPort ?for the Outport is turned 'on ', during the HDL code generation the Keyword?INOUT ?is not generated at the DUT level in the HDL code if your model is configured with?ScalarizePorts  enabled.?

Summary: MATLAB on Windows 11 hangs while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device using?ble object
Description: MATLAB installed on Windows 11 displays a busy icon and remains unresponsive while connecting to a Bluetooth Low Energy device by using the?ble ?object.

Summary: Segmentation fault when Static Code Metrics are computed
Description: A crash might occur when the Embedded Coder or MATLAB Coder requests the Static Code Metrics. The stack trace points to a line in code_info.cpp .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bus with string elements might cause wrong simulation results when used as message payload in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be wrong when a model uses a Simulink or Stateflow message and all of the following conditions are true: Message payload is a bus with string elements. Message senders and receivers (Message Send and Receive blocks or Stateflow charts) have different sample times. Model runs in rapid accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Concatenation of nested handle class data members may cause incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for MATLAB program with the following pattern:? Nested handle classes initiated with variable sized data member. Size of data member is changed through concatenating. C++ code is generated.  For example, for this MATLAB code: function result = foo()   entry = struct('f', 0);   coder.varsize('entry', [1 Inf]);   log = handleClass(entry);   x = handleClass(log);   x.contents.contents = [x.contents.contents struct('f', 1)];   result = log.contents;end MATLAB Coder generates the following incorrect code snippet:? b_log.contents.set_size(1, 1);b_log.contents[0] = r;// x.contents points to the memory of b_logx.contents = &b_log;                            // setting size of x.contents is indirectly setting size of b_log   x.contents->contents.set_size(1, b_log.contents.size(1) + 1);      loop_ub = b_log.contents.size(1);for (int32_T i{0}; i < loop_ub; i++) {  // causing indexing out-of-bounds  x.contents->contents[i] = b_log.contents[i];                     }

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for State Writer block writing to Outport block of reused conditionally executed subsystem
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model if these conditions exist: Model contains a State Writer block. State Writer block writes to an Outport block of a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, an Enabled Subsystem, Triggered Subsystem, or Function-Call Subsystem). Model is configured with these model configuration parameter settings: System target file ?is set to 'ert.tlc'. Pass reusable subsystem outputs as  is set to Individual arguments .  For conditionally executed subsystem, block parameter?Function packaging ?is set to Reusable function . Conditionally executed subsystem is inside another nonvirtual subsystem for which block parameter?Function packaging ?set to Reusable function .  When these conditions are met, the generated code for the State Writer block might write to a global variable instead of writing to the argument variable passed in to the reusable function for the subsystem, causing an incorrect answer.
Fixed In: R2021b Update 7, R2022a Update 7, R2022b Update 6, R2023a Update 1, R2023b

Summary: Generated HDL code for Reciprocal block with Shift-Add architecture and custom output types has more latency and hardware resources
Description: The HDL code generated for Reciprocal block and Math function Reciprocal block have more latency and consumes more hardware resources as compared to when using inherit via internal rule output type settings for these blocks: For Reciprocal block, Architecture  is set to?ShiftAdd   and Output data type  is other than Inherit: Inherit via internal rule .  For Math Function Reciprocal block, Architecture  is set to?ShiftAdd  and?Output data type ?is other than? Inherit: Inherit via internal rule  .

Summary: HDL Workflow Advisor errors out when using DAC Sampling frequency?greater than 7000 MHz in the IP Core Generation Reference Design Workflow
Description: While generating the reference design for ZCU111 and ZCU216 RFSoC boards, HDL Workflow Advisor generates an error in Task 1.2 Reference Design Parameters  if these conditions are true:? DAC Sampling frequency ? is greater than 7000  MHz DAC DUC Mode  is set to?IMR High-Pass (Fs/2 to Fs) .   The following error message is generated:?? ?"Maximum DAC Frequency when using FullNyquistDUC is 7000 MHz".

1] 0x00007ff84ae17338 B:\matlab\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00029496 foundation::core::diag::stacktrace_base::capture+00000024  [? 2] 0x00007ff84ae1cbc1 B:\matlab\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00052161 foundation::core::diag::symbols::getSymbolAddress+00010001  [? Summary: Compiler error in Simulink when using custom code from different directories
Description: Simulink might report a compiler error when you simulate a model that uses multiple custom code files from different directories.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b, R2024a

Summary: Error when comparing requirement set file revisions?
Description: When you compare different versions of a requirement set file with highlighting enabled, you might see an error message that says?Unable to locate Requirement Set . This occurs when a version of the requirement set that you are comparing is already opened in the Requirements Editor .
Fixed In: R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 6, R2022b Update 1, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing if conditions in the generated code with function-call model blocks with startup variant activation time
Description: The generated model step function does not contain variant conditions when all the following conditions are met :? Model contains model reference blocks containing function-call inports. This model block has startup variant activation time due to a variant block such as a Variant Source or Variant Sink model

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C3:8.6 with PaYC
Description: The MISRA C:2012 rule 8.6 checks for conflicts between multiple source files. Because Polyspace as You Code (PaYC) has knowledge of only one file at a time, this rule is not relevant to PaYC.

Summary: Parallel jobs may fail to start on Windows when the MATLAB_PREFDIR environment variable is set
Description: Parallel jobs may fail to start or error when all of the following conditions are true:? The workers are running on Windows. Multiple workers are starting at the same time, for example through parpool()  or batch(..,'Pool',N) .  The environment variable MATLAB_PREFDIR is set.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystems with feedback loops might cause a numeric mismatch between the original and generated model when resource sharing is enabled and latency is introduced upstream
Description: This report builds on top of?https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/2226678 . In addition to the issues mentioned in the report above: In R2021b, if resource sharing is enabled for the subsystem with a feedback loop, the same issue may occur.
Workaround: The same workaround in the linked bug report above can apply here.In addition to those, removing the resource sharing request in R2021b might result in the mismatch being fixed.?

Summary: Error when generating software interface model from linked library block in the IP Core generation workflow
Description: HDL Coder throws an error while generating Software Interface Model  in the IP Core Generation workflow, if your model contains a DUT with linked subsystem. The following error message is generated: Failed Attempt to modify block in a linked subsystem.  This can only be done by the block or its parent through their mask initialization code.

Summary: Uncompilable code in model reference hierarchy when Block Parameter is a MATLAB Structure
Description: Simulink might fail to compile a model reference hierarchy under these conditions:?? A model in the hierarchy uses variables with structured data types without a bus object. A model at a higher level in the hierarchy uses Simulink.Bus  objects with identical field names and field data types.  An error message similar to the following might be issued in this case:? '$(variable name)' : redefinition; different basic types
Workaround: Declare the workspace variable as a Simulink.Parameter object with the expected Simulink.Bus type instead of a MATLAB structure variable.

Summary: Python interpreter is loaded during static name resolution
Description: The Python interpreter configured for MATLAB might have been already loaded before the invocation of any Python functionality from MATLAB. For example, setting a breakpoint in MATLAB editor for a MATLAB function that contains a Python interface statement such as?py.int ?can load the Python interpreter.
Workaround: Wrap all calls to the Python interface with the?eval?function. For the example, if you have the following MATLAB function myFunc:function ret=myFunc? ? ?ret=py.int(5);end?Update the code with:?function ret=myFunc? ? ?ret=eval('py.int(5)');end?
